## Integer Type Questions

1. Ans : 3
$\pm \sqrt{3}$ are zeroes $\Rightarrow(x+\sqrt{3})(x-\sqrt{3})=x^{2}-3$ is a divisor of $x^{4}-3 x^{3}-7 x^{2}+9 x+12$.

$$
\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm { x } ^ { 2 } - 3 \longdiv { x ^ { 4 } - 3 x ^ { 3 } - 7 x ^ { 2 } + 9 x + 1 2 } ( x ^ { 2 } - 3 x - 4 \\
& \frac{x^{4}+3 x^{2}}{-3 \mathrm{x}^{3}-4 \mathrm{x}^{2}+9 \mathrm{x}+12} \\
& \frac{-3 \mathrm{x}^{2}+9 \mathrm{x}}{+\mathrm{x}^{2}+12} \\
& q(x)=x^{2}-3 x-\frac{-4 \mathrm{x}^{2}+12}{+-4=x^{2}-4 x+x-4}
\end{aligned}
$$

$$
\begin{aligned}
& \quad=x(x-4)+1(x-4) \\
& q(x)=(x+1)(x-4) \\
& \text { zeroes of } q(x) \text { are }-1,4
\end{aligned}
$$

∴ Remaining required zeroes are $-1,4$.
Sum $=-1+4=3$
2. Ans : 8

Let $\alpha$ and $\beta$ are zeroes of quadratic polynomial
$p(s)=3 s^{2}-6 s+4$
So, $\alpha+\beta=-\left(\frac{-6}{3}\right)=2$ and $\alpha \beta=4 / 3$
Let $x=\frac{\alpha}{\beta}+\frac{\beta}{\alpha}+2\left(\frac{1}{\alpha}+\frac{1}{\beta}\right)+3 \alpha \beta$
$=\frac{\alpha^{2}+\beta^{2}}{\alpha \beta}+2\left(\frac{\alpha+\beta}{\alpha \beta}\right)+3 \alpha \beta$
We know, $\alpha^{2}+\beta^{2}=(\alpha+\beta)^{2}-2 \alpha \beta=2^{2}-2 \times \frac{4}{3}$
$=4-\frac{8}{3}=\frac{4}{3}$

$$
\begin{aligned}
\alpha^{2}+\beta^{2}=\frac{4}{3}, \alpha+\beta & =2 \text { and } \alpha \beta=4 / 3 . \text { So, } x=\frac{4 / 3}{4 / 3} \\
& +2\left(\frac{2}{4 / 3}\right)+3 \times \frac{4}{3}=1+4 \times \frac{3}{4}+4=8
\end{aligned}
$$

3. Ans: 7

If $x^{4}+x^{3}+8 x^{2}+a x+b$ is exactly divisible by $x^{2}+1$, then the remainder should be zero.
On dividing the polynomial by $x^{2}+1$, we get,

$$
\begin{gathered}
x ^ { 2 } + 1 \longdiv { x ^ { 4 } + x ^ { 3 } + 8 x ^ { 2 } + a x + b } x ^ { 2 } + x + 7 \\
\frac{-x^{4}+x^{2}}{x^{3}+7 x^{2}+a x+b} \\
\frac{-x^{3}+x}{7 x^{2}+x(a-1)+b} \\
\frac{-7 x^{2}+7}{x(a-1)+b-7}
\end{gathered}
$$

$\therefore \quad$ Quotient $=x^{2}+x+7$ and
Remainder $=x(a-1)+(2-7)$
Now, since, $x^{2}+1$ is factor of this polynomial,
$\therefore \quad$ Remainder $=0$
$\Rightarrow x(a-1)+(b-7)=0 \Rightarrow x(a-1)+(b-7)=0 x+0$
Equating coefficient of $x$ and constant term, we get,
$a-1=0$ and $b-7=0 \Rightarrow a=1$ and $\mathrm{b}=7$
$\therefore a b=1 \times 7=7$
4. Ans : 2
$\alpha+\beta=-\frac{5}{2}$ and $\alpha \beta=\frac{k}{2}$
$\alpha^{2}+\beta^{2}+\alpha \beta=\frac{21}{4}\left[\because \alpha^{2}+\beta^{2}=(\alpha+\beta)^{2}-2 \alpha \beta\right]$
$\Rightarrow(\alpha+\beta)^{2}-\alpha \beta=\frac{21}{4} \Rightarrow \frac{25}{4}-\frac{k}{2}=\frac{21}{4} \Rightarrow k=2$

![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-060.jpg?height=378&width=461&top_left_y=396&top_left_x=1354)
Foundation

## (CONCEPT M A <br> A P )

![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-060.jpg?height=762&width=891&top_left_y=1788&top_left_x=322)

| Algebraic Method |
| :--- |
| Consider the following system of equation <br> There are following three methods under Algebraic method to solve the above system: <br> (i) Substitution method <br> (a) Find the value of one variable, say y in terms of x or x in terms of y <br> (b) Substitute this value in second equation to get equation in one variable <br> (c) Now substitute the value/solution so obtained in the equation got <br> (ii) Elimination Method <br> Write equation as $a_{1} x+b_{1} y+c_{1}=0 ; a_{2} x+b_{2} y+c_{2}=0$ from one equation. and find solution. in step(a) <br> (a) If coefficient of one variable are not same in both the equation multiply both the equation with suitable non-zero constants to make coefficient of one variable numerically equal. <br> (b) Add or subtract the equation to get equation in one variable and solve it. <br> (c) Now substitute the value got in the above step in either of the original equation to get value of the other variable. <br> (iii) Cross multiplication method <br> Consider the following diagram.![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-060.jpg?height=468&width=125&top_left_y=904&top_left_x=974) $\frac{x}{b_{1} c_{2}-b_{2} c_{1}}=\frac{y}{c_{1} a_{2}-c_{2} a_{1}}=\frac{1}{a_{1} b_{2}-a_{2} b_{1}}$ <br> Solve it to get the solution provided, $a_{1} b_{2}-a_{2} b_{1} \neq 0$ |


| Graphical Method |
| :--- |
| Consider the standard form of linear equations in two variables. $a_{1} x+b_{1} y+c_{1}=0 ; a_{2} x+b_{2} y+c_{2}=0$ <br> While solving the above system of equation by this method following three cases arise. <br> (i) If $\frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}} \neq \frac{b_{1}}{b_{2}}$; system is called consistent, having one or unique solution and pair of straight lines representing the above equations intersect at one point only. <br> (ii) If $\frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}}=\frac{b_{1}}{b_{2}}=\frac{c_{1}}{c_{2}}$; system is called dependent and have infinitely many solution. Pair of lines representing the equations coincide. <br> (iii) If $\frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}}=\frac{b_{1}}{b_{2}} \neq \frac{c_{1}}{c_{2}}$; system is called inconsistent and has no solution. Pair of lines representing the equations are parallel or do not intersect at any point. |

## Equations Reducible to a Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables

By using the suitable substitution or simplification first we convert the given system into the system of a pair of linear equations in two variables. Then after using any algebraic or graphical method we solve the system.

## SOLUTION OF A PAIR OF LINEAR EQUATIONS IN TWO VARIABLES

To find two numbers such that their sum is 35 and their difference is 5 . We can represent the problem algebraically by letting $x$ represents the large number and $y$ represents the smaller number. The problem is indicated by the two equations as given below:

$$
\begin{aligned}
& x+y=33 \\
& x-y=5
\end{aligned}
$$

The two equations for which we seek a common solution are called simultaneous equations. The two equations, taken together, comprise a system of equations. The value of the variables $x$ and $y$ that satisfied the above system of equations is called its solution. Each of the above equations represents a straight line.
Graphically solution of a system of a pair of linear equations in two variables is the coordinates of the point where the two lines intersect each other.

## CONSISTENT, DEPENDENT AND INCONSISTENT SYSTEM OF EQUATIONS

System of a pair of linear equations in two variables :

$$
\begin{aligned}
& a_{1} x+b_{1} y+c_{1}=0 \\
& a_{2} x+b_{2} y+c_{2}=0
\end{aligned}
$$

The given system of a pair of linear equations in two variables has either one solution, infinite solutions or no solution. Note that the variables in both the equations are same.
Case (i); If $\frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}} \neq \frac{b_{1}}{b_{2}}$, then the system has only one (or unique) solution, and the system is called consistent.
In this case, a pair of straight lines represented by the system of equations intersect each other exactly at one point.
Example: Consider the system of linear equations $2 x-3 y=6$ and $x+y=1$
Here, $a_{1}=2, a_{2}=1, b_{1}=-3, b_{2}=1$
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-061.jpg?height=254&width=170&top_left_y=1343&top_left_x=1701)

Now, $\frac{2}{1} \neq \frac{-3}{1} \Rightarrow \frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}} \neq \frac{b_{1}}{b_{2}}$
Intersecting lines
So, the system has unique solution.
Case (ii); If $\frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}}=\frac{b_{1}}{b_{2}}=\frac{c_{1}}{c_{2}}$, then the given system has infinite many solutions and the system is called dependent.
In this case, a pair of lines represented by the system of equations, coincides with each other.
So they intersect each other at infinite number of points.
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-061.jpg?height=61&width=227&top_left_y=1829&top_left_x=1176)

Example: Consider the system of equations $3 x-y+5=0$ and $6 x-2 y+10=0$
Here, $a_{1}=3, a_{2}=6, b_{1}=-1, b_{2}=-2, c_{1}=5, c_{2}=10$
Now, $\frac{3}{6}=\frac{-1}{-2}=\frac{5}{10} \Rightarrow \frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}}=\frac{b_{1}}{b_{2}}=\frac{c_{1}}{c_{2}}$
Now, the system has infinite solutions.
Case (iii); If $\frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}}=\frac{b_{1}}{b_{2}} \neq \frac{c_{1}}{c_{2}}$, then the given system has no solution and the system is called inconsistent. In this case, a pair of lines represented by the system are parallel to each other.
So they do not intersect each other at any point.
Example: Consider the system of equations $x-5 y-6=0$ and $2 x-10 y-14=0$
Here, $a_{1}=1, a_{2}=2, b_{1}=-5, b_{2}=-10, c_{1}=-6, c_{2}=-14$
Now, $\frac{1}{2}=\frac{-5}{-10} \neq \frac{-6}{-14} \Rightarrow \frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}}=\frac{b_{1}}{b_{2}} \neq \frac{c_{1}}{c_{2}}$
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-061.jpg?height=278&width=183&top_left_y=2133&top_left_x=1688)

Hence, the system has no solution.

## GRAPHICAL METHOD TO SOLVE A PAIR OF LINEAR EQUATIONS IN TWO VARIABLES

Draw the graph of both the equations of a pair of equations in two variables using same pair of horizontal and vertical lines called X and Y -axis respectively. Coordinates of the point(s) of intersection of the two lines is/are the solution(s) of the given pair of linear equations in two variables.

## ILLUSTRATION : 1

Check whether the pair of equations

$$
x+3 y=6
$$

and

$$
2 x-3 y=12
$$

is consistent. If so, solve them graphically.

## SOLUTION :

Here, $\frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}}=\frac{1}{2}, \frac{b_{1}}{b_{2}}=\frac{3}{-3}=-1, \therefore \frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}} \neq \frac{b_{1}}{b_{2}}$
Hence, the given system of a pair of equations in two variable is consistent.
Let us draw the graphs of the equations (i) and (ii). For this, we find two solutions of each of the equations, which are given below in the table.
$x+3 y=6$

| $\boldsymbol{x}$ | 0 | 6 |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| $\boldsymbol{y}$ | 2 | 0 |

$2 x-3 y=12$

| $\boldsymbol{x}$ | 0 | 3 |
| :---: | :---: | :---: |
| $\boldsymbol{y}$ | -4 | -2 |

Plot the points $A(0,2), B(6,0), P(0,-4)$ and $Q(3,-2)$ on graph paper, and join the points to form the lines $A B$ and $P Q$.
Point $B(6,0)$ common to both the lines $A B$ and $P Q$. So, the solution of the pair of linear equations
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-062.jpg?height=716&width=913&top_left_y=877&top_left_x=904) is $x=6$ and $y=0$.

## ILLUSTRATION : $\mathbf{2}$

In each of the following, find whether the system is consistent, inconsistent or dependent :
(i) $5 x+2 y+16=0$
(ii) $\quad 5 x+2 y+16=0$
(iii) $\quad 5 x+2 y+16=0$
$7 x-4 y+2=0$
$3 x+\frac{6}{5} y+2=0$

$$
\frac{15}{2} x+3 y+24=0
$$

## SOLUTION :

(i) $\frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}}=\frac{5}{7}, \frac{b_{1}}{b_{2}}=\frac{2}{-4}=-\frac{1}{2}$

$$
\because \frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}} \neq \frac{b_{1}}{b_{2}}
$$

Hence, the given system is consistent.
(ii) $\frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}}=\frac{5}{3}, \frac{b_{1}}{b_{2}}=\frac{10}{6}=\frac{5}{3}, \frac{c_{1}}{c_{2}}=\frac{16}{2}=\frac{8}{1}$
$\because \frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}}=\frac{b_{1}}{b_{2}} \neq \frac{c_{1}}{c_{2}}$
Hence, the given system is inconsistent.
(iii) $\frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}}=\frac{10}{15}=\frac{2}{3}, \frac{b_{1}}{b_{2}}=\frac{2}{3}, \frac{c_{1}}{c_{2}}=\frac{16}{24}=\frac{2}{3}$
$\because \frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}}=\frac{b_{1}}{b_{2}}=\frac{c_{1}}{c_{2}}$
Therefore, the given system is dependent.

## ALGEBRAIC METHODS OF SOLVING A PAIR OF CONSISTENT LINEAR EQUATIONS

A system of a pair of linear equations can be solved algebraically by following three methods.
(A) Substitution Method
(B) Elimination Method
(C) Cross-multiplication Method

## (A) First Method - Substitution Method

In substitution method, you have to express one of the variables in terms of the other using either of the equations and substitute this in other equation.

## Algorithm to find the solution :

A system of a pair of linear equations in two variables:

$$
\begin{aligned}
& a_{1} x+b_{1} y+c_{1}=0 \\
& a_{2} x+b_{2} y+c_{2}=0
\end{aligned}
$$

Step 1 : Find the value of one variable, say $y$ in terms of the other variable, i.e., $x$ from either equation, whichever is convenient. Consider this equation as equation (iii).
Step 2 : Substitute this value of $y$ getting from equation (iii) in either equation (i) or (ii). This gives an equation in one variable $x$, from which you can easily get the value of $x$.
Step 3 : Substitute the value of $x$ obtained in Step 2 in the equations (iii), and obtain the value of the other variable $y$.

## ILLUSTRATION : 3

## Find the solution of the following system by substitution method :

$$
4 x+y=11 ; x+2 y=8
$$

## SOLUTION :

$$
\begin{aligned}
& 4 x+y=11 \\
& x+2 y=8
\end{aligned}
$$

It is easy to find the value of $y$ in terms of $x$ from the equation or to find the value of $x$ in terms of $y$ from the second equation. Let us find the value of $y$ in terms of $x$ from the first equation. The result is $y=11-4 x$

Now putting the value of $y$ in equation (ii), we get

$$
x+2(11-4 x)=8
$$

We now have one equation that is linear in $x$; i.e the equation contains only the variable $x$.
On solving, we find that

$$
x+22-8 x=8 \quad \text { or } \quad-7 x=8-22 \quad \text { or } \quad-7 x=-14 \quad \text { or } \quad x=2
$$

To get the corresponding value of $y$, we substitute $x=2$ in equation (iii). The result is

$$
y=11-4(2)=11-8=3
$$

Thus, the solution of the given pair of linear equations are $x=2$ and $y=3$.

## (B) Second Method - Elimination Method

## Algorithm to find the solution:

Step 1 : If coefficients of one variable (either $x$ or $y$ ) in both the equations are equal, then go to step- 2 otherwise multiply one or both the equation(s) by some suitable non-zero constants to make the coefficients of one variable (either $x$ or $y$ ) numerically equal.
Step 2 : Add one equation to other or subtract one equation from the other so that one variable gets eliminated. In this case, you get an equation in one variable. By solving this equation you can easily get the value of the variable involved in this equation.
Step 3 : Substitute the value of the variable ( $x$ or $y$ ) obtained in step- 2 in either of the original equations to get the value of the other variable.
The elimination method is preferred when the numerical coefficients of one variable in the original equations are same.

## ILLUSTRATIOM : 4

The ratio of incomes of two persons is 9:7 and the ratio of their expenditures is $4: 3$. If each of them manages to save ₹ $\mathbf{2 0 0 0}$ per month, find their monthly incomes.

## SOLUTION :

Let the income of the two person be $₹ 9 x$ and $₹ 7 x$ and their expenditures be $₹ 4 y$ and $₹ 3 y$ respectively. Then the equations formed for the given situation are given by :

$$
\text { and } \quad \begin{aligned}
& 9 x-4 y=2000 \\
& 7 x-3 y=2000
\end{aligned}
$$

Step 1 : Multiply equation (i) by 3 and Equation (ii) by 4 to make the coefficients of $y$ equal. Then, we get the equations:

$$
\begin{aligned}
& 27 x-12 y=6000 \\
& 28 x-12 y=8000
\end{aligned}
$$

Step 2 : Subtract equation (iii) from equation (iv) to eliminate $y$, because the coefficients of $y$ are the same in equation (iii) and (iv). So, we get

$$
\begin{aligned}
& (28 x-27 x)-(12 y-12 y)=8000-6000 \\
\Rightarrow \quad & x=2000
\end{aligned}
$$

Step 3 : Substituting this value of $x$ in (i), we get

$$
9(2000)-4 y=2000 \quad \text { i.e., } \quad y=4000
$$

So, the solution of the equations: $x=2000, y=4000$. Therefore, the monthly incomes of the two persons are ₹ 18,000 and ₹ 14,000 , respectively.
Verification : $18000: 14000=9: 7$.
Also, the ratio of their expenditures $=18000-2000: 14000-2000=16000: 12000=4: 3$

## (C) Third Method - Cross-multiplication Method

## Algorithm to find the solution:

Step 1: Write the given equations in the form :

$$
\begin{aligned}
& a_{1} x+b_{1} y+c_{1}=0 \\
& a_{2} x+b_{2} y+c_{2}=0
\end{aligned}
$$

Step 2 : Taking the help of the diagram given below :
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-064.jpg?height=326&width=1111&top_left_y=906&top_left_x=450)

The arrows between the two numbers indicate that they are to be multiplied and the second product is to be subtracted from the first product.
Write equation as

$$
\frac{x}{b_{1} c_{2}-b_{2} c_{1}}=\frac{y}{c_{1} a_{2}-c_{2} a_{1}}=\frac{1}{a_{1} b_{2}-a_{2} b_{1}}
$$

Step 3 : If $a_{1} b_{2}-a_{2} b_{1} \neq 0$, then solve $\frac{x}{b_{1} c_{2}-b_{2} c_{1}}=\frac{1}{a_{1} b_{2}-a_{2} b_{1}}$ for $x$ and solve $\frac{y}{c_{1} a_{2}-c_{2} a_{1}}=\frac{1}{a_{1} b_{2}-a_{2} b_{1}}$ for $y$.

## ILLUSTRATION : 5

Solve the following equations by cross-multiplication method:

$$
3 x+2 y+25=0, x+y+15=0
$$

## SOLUTION :

$$
\begin{aligned}
& 3 x+2 y+25=0 \\
& x+y+15=0
\end{aligned}
$$

Here,

$$
\begin{aligned}
& a_{1}=3, b_{1}=2, c_{1}=25 \\
& a_{2}=1, b_{2}=1, c_{2}=15
\end{aligned}
$$

∴
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-064.jpg?height=266&width=905&top_left_y=1914&top_left_x=395)

$$
\begin{aligned}
& \frac{x}{2 \times 15-25 \times 1}=\frac{y}{25 \times 1-15 \times 3}=\frac{1}{3 \times 1-2 \times 1} \Rightarrow \frac{x}{30-25}=\frac{y}{25-45}=\frac{1}{3-2} \\
& \Rightarrow \frac{x}{5}=\frac{y}{-20}=\frac{1}{1} \Rightarrow \frac{x}{5}=1, \frac{y}{-20}=1 \Rightarrow x=5, y=-20
\end{aligned}
$$

![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-065.jpg?height=150&width=346&top_left_y=374&top_left_x=255)

- In a cyclic quadrilateral $\mathrm{ABCD}, \angle \mathrm{A}=(x+2)^{\circ}, \angle \mathrm{B}=(y+3)^{\circ}, \angle \mathrm{C}=(3 y+8)^{\circ}$ and $\angle \mathrm{D}=(4 x-8)^{\circ}$, find four angles.


## SOLUTION

In a cylic quadrilateral $\mathrm{ABCD}, \angle \mathrm{A}+\angle \mathrm{C}=180^{\circ}$ and $\angle \mathrm{B}+\angle \mathrm{D}=180^{\circ}$
[sum of opposite angles of a cyclic quadrilateral is $180^{\circ}$ ]
$\Rightarrow(x+2)^{\circ}+(3 y+8)^{\circ}=180^{\circ}$ and $(y+3)^{\circ}+(4 x-8)^{\circ}=180^{\circ}$
$\Rightarrow x+3 y=170$
... (i) and $4 x+y=185$

Using elimination method, multiply equation (i) by (4), we get
$4 x+12 y=4 \times 170$

On subtracting equation (iii) from (ii), we get $-11 y=-495 \Rightarrow y=45^{\circ}$ and $x=35^{\circ}$
$\therefore \quad \angle \mathrm{A}=(35+2)^{\circ}=37^{\circ} ; \quad \angle \mathrm{B}=(45+3)^{\circ}=48^{\circ}$
$\angle \mathrm{C}=(3 \times 45+8)^{\circ}=143^{\circ}$ and $\angle \mathrm{D}=(4 \times 35-8)^{\circ}=132^{\circ}$

## SOLUTION OF A SYSTEM OF A PAIR OF EQUATIONS REDUCIBLE TO THE SYSTEM OF A PAIR OF LINEAR EQUATIONS IN TWO VARIABLES

By using the suitable substitution or simplification first we convert the given system into the system of a pair of linear equations in two variables. Then after using any algebraic or graphical method we solve the system.

## ILLUSTRATIOM : $\mathbf{6}$

Solve the equations : $\frac{2 x+1}{3}+\frac{3 y+2}{5}=2$ and $\frac{2(2 x+1)}{3}-\frac{3(3 y+2)}{5}=-1$

## SOLUTION :

Given equations are : $\frac{2 x+1}{3}+\frac{3 y+2}{5}=2$
...(i) and $\frac{2(2 x+1)}{3}-\frac{3(3 y+2)}{5}=-1$

Let $\frac{2 x+1}{3}=u$ and $\frac{3 y+2}{5}=v$
Then the equations become

$$
u+v=2 \quad \ldots \text { (iii) } \quad 2 u-3 v=-1
$$

Multiplying (iii) by 3, $3 u+3 v=6$.

Adding (iv) and (v), $5 u=5 \Rightarrow u=1$
Substituting this value of $u$ in (iii), $1+v=2 \Rightarrow v=2-1=1$
Then $\frac{2 x+1}{3}=u=1$ and $\frac{3 y+2}{5}=v=1 \Rightarrow x=1 \quad$ and $y=1$

## SOLVING THE WORD PROBLEMS

Many problems can be solved quickly and easily by converting them into a system of a pair of linear equations in two variables as follows :
(i) Represent the unknown quantities by any two variables say $x$ and $y$, which are to be determined.
(ii) Find the conditions given in the problem and translate the verbal conditions into a pair of simultaneous linear equations using the variables $x$ and $y$.
(iii) Solve these equations and obtain the required quantities with appropriate units.

## ILLUSTRATIOM : 7

Find the two numbers such that half of the first equals one third of the second and twice their sum exceeds three times the second by 4 .

## SOLUTION :

Let $x$ and $y$ be the first and second numbers respectively. We can write two equations almost directly from the statements of the problem as $\frac{x}{2}=\frac{y}{3}$
.... (i) and

$$
2(x+y)=3 y+4
$$

Find the value of $x$ in terms of $y$ from first equation, $x=\frac{2 y}{3}$

Now putting the value of $x$ in terms of $y$ in second equation, $2\left(\frac{2 y}{3}+y\right)=3 y+4$
$\Rightarrow \frac{4 y}{3}+2 y=3 y+4 \Rightarrow 4 y+6 y=9 y+12 \Rightarrow y=12$ (second number)
Now putting the value of $x$ in equation (i), $\frac{x}{2}=\frac{12}{3} \Rightarrow x=8$ (first number)

## APPLICATIONS OF A SYSTEM OF A PAIR OF LINEAR EQUATIONS IN TWO VARIABLES

It is used to solve real life problem having two unknown by converting the real life problem into a pair of linear equations. It will be more clear by the following Illustration.

## ILLUSTRATION : 8

On a national highway, place $\boldsymbol{P}_{1}$ and $\boldsymbol{P}_{2}$ are 250 km apart from each other.
A car starts from $\boldsymbol{P}_{1}$ \& another from $\boldsymbol{P}_{2}$ at the same time. If they go in the same direction then they meet in 5 hours and if they go in opposite directions they meet in $\frac{\mathbf{2 5}}{\mathbf{1 3}}$ hours. Find their speeds.

## SOLUTION :

Let $A$ and $B$ be the two cars. $A$ starts from $P_{1}$ with constant speed of $x \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{hr}$ and $B$ starts from $P_{2}$ with constant speed $y \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{hr}$.
Case I: When the two cars move in same direction as shown in figures, the cars meet at position $Q$.
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-066.jpg?height=110&width=1186&top_left_y=1181&top_left_x=411)

Here, $P_{1} Q=5 x \mathrm{~km}$ i.e., the distance travelled by car $A$ in 5 hours with $x \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{hr}$.
and $P_{2} Q=5 y \mathrm{~km}$ i.e. the distance travelled by car $B$ in 5 hours with $y \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{hr}$.
We have, $P, Q-P_{2} Q=250 \Rightarrow 5 x-5 y=250 \Rightarrow x-y=50$

Case II: When two cars move in opposite direction as shown in figure, the cars meet at the position $R$.
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-066.jpg?height=108&width=1250&top_left_y=1480&top_left_x=379)

Here, $P_{1} R=\frac{25}{13} x \mathrm{~km}$ and $P_{2} R=\frac{25}{13} y \mathrm{~km}$
So, $P_{1} R+P_{2} R=250 \Rightarrow \frac{25}{13} x+\frac{25}{13} y=250 \quad \Rightarrow \quad x+y=130$

Adding (i) and (ii), we get ; $2 x=180 \Rightarrow x=90$ and $y=130-90=40$
Speed of car $A=90 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{hr}$ and speed of car $B=40 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{hr}$

| ![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-067.jpg?height=1458&width=61&top_left_y=690&top_left_x=262) |  |

![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-068.jpg?height=61&width=142&top_left_y=2239&top_left_x=297)
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-068.jpg?height=80&width=142&top_left_y=2126&top_left_x=297)
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-068.jpg?height=72&width=156&top_left_y=2025&top_left_x=283)
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-068.jpg?height=151&width=161&top_left_y=1852&top_left_x=284)

- If $x$ years be the sum of ages of a group of $y$ persons, then the sum of ages of these persons after $t$ years will be $(x+y t)$ years, because after $t$ years, age of each person in the group is increased by $t$ years and hence age of $y$ persons is increased by $y t$ years.
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-068.jpg?height=634&width=189&top_left_y=1758&top_left_x=590)
- If $x$ years be the sum of ages of a group of $y$ persons, then the sum of ages of these $y$ persons before $t$ years will be $(x-y t)$ years, because before $t$ years age of each person in the group is decreased by $t$ years and hence age of $y$ persons is decreased by $y t$ years.
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-068.jpg?height=629&width=222&top_left_y=1763&top_left_x=928)
- If the average age of a group of persons is $x$ years, then after $t$ years, their average age will be $(x+t)$ years and before $t$ years, their average age will be ( $x-t$ ) years.

![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-068.jpg?height=718&width=199&top_left_y=1716&top_left_x=1264)
[Average age $=x$ years]

- When in the question, comparison between ages after some years or before some years are given and you have to assume these ages, then you first assume their present ages and then accordingly find their ages after or before some years.
Let us understand the above concepts in a better way in the different cases through illustrative examples.


## CASE STUDY-2 : Based on Ages

CASE-I : Ages of a group of persons after some years:
Illustration: Father's age is three times the sum of ages of his two children. After 7 years, his age will be twice the sum of ages of two children. Find the present age of father. Sol. Let sum of ages of two children be $x$ years and father's age be $y$ years.
$\therefore \quad y=3 x \ldots$ (i) After 7 years, Father's age $=(y+7)$ years
Sum of ages of two children $=x+2 \times 7=(x+14)$ years $\therefore y+7=2(x+14)$
$\Rightarrow \quad y=2 x+21 \quad \ldots$ (ii)
on solving eqns. (i) and (ii), we get Father's age $=y=63$ years
CASE-II : Ages of a group of persons before some years.
Illustration: Age of Aman is 5 years less than twice the sum of ages of his two daughters and a son. Six years ago, his age was three times the sum of ages of his two daughters and a son. Find the sum of present ages of Aman's three children's.

Sol. Let sum of ages of two daughters and a son be $x$ years and Aman's age be $y$ years. $\therefore y=2 x-5 \quad \ldots$ (i) Six years ago, Sum of ages of two daughters and a son $=x-3 \times 6 =(x-18)$ years Aman's age $=(y-6)$ years ∴ $y-6=3(x-18) \quad \ldots$ (ii) on solving equations (i) and (ii), we get Sum of ages of three children's $=x=43$ years

CASE-III : Average age of a group of persons before and after some years. Illustration: 5 years hence, Krishana's age will be twice the average age of his friend's family. 5 years ago, he was three times older than the average age of his friend's family. Find present average age of his friend's family.

Sol. Let Krishana's present age be $x$ years and his friend's family present average age be $y$ years. 5 years hence, Krishana's age $=(x+5)$ years

Average age of friend's family $=(y+5)$ years ∴ $\quad x+5=2(y+5)$

5 years ago, Krishana's age $=(x-5)$ years
Average age of friend's family $=(y-5)$ years $\quad \therefore \quad x-5=3(y-5)$
on solving equation (i) and (ii), we get
Krishana's friend's family present average age $=y=5$ years.

## MISCELLANEOUS SOLVED EXAMPLES

1. Solve for $x$ and $y: \frac{3}{x}+\frac{4}{y}=1 ; \frac{4}{x}+\frac{2}{y}=\frac{11}{12}$

Sol. $\frac{3}{x}+\frac{4}{y}=1$

$$
\frac{4}{x}+\frac{2}{y}=\frac{11}{12}
$$

Multiplying (ii) by 2 to make coefficient of $y$ same in both equations $\Rightarrow \frac{8}{x}+\frac{4}{y}=\frac{22}{12}$

Subtracting (iii) from (i) equation $\Rightarrow \frac{5}{x}=\frac{10}{12}, \therefore x=\frac{5 \times 12}{10}=6$
Substituting $x=6$ in (i), we get
$\Rightarrow \frac{3}{6}+\frac{4}{y}=1 \Rightarrow \frac{4}{y}=1-\frac{1}{2}=\frac{1}{2} \quad \therefore \quad y=8$.
Hence, $x=6$ and $y=8$
2. The sum of a two-digit number and the number obtained by reversing the digits is $\mathbf{6 6}$. If the digits of the number differ by 2 , find the number. How many such numbers are there?
Sol. Let the ten's and the unit's digits in the first number be $x$ and $y$, respectively.
So, the first number may be written as $10 x+y$ in the expanded form (for example, $56=10(5)+6$ ).
When the digits are reversed, $x$ becomes the unit's digit and $y$ becomes the ten's digit. This number, in the expanded notation is $10 y+x$ (for example, when 56 is reversed, we get $65=10(6)+5$ ).
According to the given condition.
$(10 x+y)+(10 y+x)=66 \quad$ i.e., $11(x+y)=66$
i.e., $x+y=6$

We are also given that the digits differ by 2 , therefore,
either

$$
\begin{gathered}
x-y=2 \\
y-x=2
\end{gathered}
$$

or

If $x-y=2$, then solving (i) and (ii) by elimination, we get $x=4$ and $y=2$. In this case, we get the number 42 .
If $y-x=2$, then solving (i) and (iii) by elimination, we get $x=2$ and $y=4$. In this case, we get the number 24 .
Thus, there are two such numbers 42 and 24 .
Verification : Here, $42+24=66$ and $4-2=2$. Also $24+42=66$ and $4-2=2$.
3. Solve the following system of equations graphically:

$$
\begin{aligned}
& 2 x+y=3 \\
& 2 x-3 y=7
\end{aligned}
$$

Identify whether these two lines are coincident or parallel. Also, find the co-ordinates of the point where any of the lines cut Y-axis.
Sol. From equation (i) :
$2 x+y=3 \Rightarrow y=3-2 x$

| $\boldsymbol{x}$ | 0 | 1 | -1 | 2 |
| :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: |
| $\boldsymbol{y}$ | 3 | 1 | 5 | -1 |

From equation (ii) : $2 x-3 y=7 \Rightarrow 3 y=2 x-7$
$\therefore y=\frac{2 x-7}{3}$

| $\boldsymbol{x}$ | 2 | 3.5 | 5 |
| :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: |
| $\boldsymbol{y}$ | -1 | 0 | 1 |

[Note: To draw a line we need to find minimum two points. We choose values for $x$ such that it gives integral values of $y$.]
The graphs of the lines are plotted as shown below:
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-069.jpg?height=547&width=611&top_left_y=1834&top_left_x=1247)

Two lines are intersecting line with a unique solution $(2,-1)$.
[To determine the point of intersection of two lines. Draw perpendicular on X and Y axis from the point of intersection of two lines.]
Line $2 x+y=3$ cuts Y-axis at ( 0,3 )
4. For what values of $k$ will the following pair of linear equations have infinitely many solutions?

$$
\begin{aligned}
& k x+3 y-(k-3)=0 \\
& 12 x+k y-k=0
\end{aligned}
$$

Sol. Comparing the given equations with $a_{1} x+b_{1} y+c_{1}=0$ and $a_{2} x+b_{2} y+c_{2}=0$
We get, $\frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}}=\frac{k}{12}, \frac{b_{1}}{b_{2}}=\frac{3}{k}, \frac{c_{1}}{c_{2}}=\frac{k-3}{k}$. For the pair of linear equations to have infinitely many solutions:
Condition $\frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}}=\frac{b_{1}}{b_{2}}=\frac{c_{1}}{c_{2}}$ must satisfy. So, we have $\frac{k}{12}=\frac{3}{k}=\frac{k-3}{k} \Rightarrow \frac{k}{12}=\frac{3}{k}$ which gives $k^{2}=36$ i.e., $k= \pm 6$
Also, $\frac{3}{k}=\frac{k-3}{k}$ gives $3 k=k^{2}-3 k$, i.e., $6 k=k^{2} \Rightarrow k^{2}-6 k=0 \Rightarrow k(k-6)=0$, which means $k=0$ or $k=6$.
Therefore, the value of $k$, that satisfies both the conditions, is $k=6$. For this value, the pair of linear equations has infinitely many solutions.
5. Draw the graph of the given pair of linear equations : $x+3 y=6$ and $2 x-3 y=12$

Hence, find the area of the region bounded by $x=0, y=0$ and $2 x-3 y=12$
Sol. Consider both the equations, separately.

$$
\begin{array}{l|r}
x+3 y=6 & 2 x-3 y=12 \\
x=6-3 y & 2 x=3 y+12 \\
x=\frac{3 y+12}{2}
\end{array}
$$

We make the tables by giving the values to $x$ for the both equations separately.
$x=6-3 y$

$$
x=\frac{3 y-12}{2}
$$

| $\boldsymbol{x}$ | 0 | 6 | 3 |
| :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: |
| $\boldsymbol{y}$ | 2 | 0 | 1 |


| $\boldsymbol{x}$ | 0 | 6 | 3 |
| :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: |
| $\boldsymbol{y}$ | -4 | 0 | -2 |

![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-070.jpg?height=467&width=757&top_left_y=1005&top_left_x=1050)

After plotting the points on the graph and joining them we get that the lines intersect at $A(6,0)$
By joining the lines and points we get a $\triangle A B C$ with vertices $A(6,0), B(0,-4), C(0,2)$. But $x=0, y=0$ and $2 x-3 y=12$ gives us $\triangle O A B$.

$$
\begin{aligned}
\therefore \text { Area of } \triangle O A B= & \frac{1}{2} \times O A \times O B \quad\left[\because \text { Area of } \Delta=\frac{1}{2} \times \text { base × corresponding altitude }\right] \\
& =\frac{1}{2} \times 6 \times 4=12 \text { sq. units }
\end{aligned}
$$

6. A boat goes 12 km upstream and 40 km downstream in 8 hours. It can go 16 km upstream and 32 km downstream in the same time. Find the speed of the boat in still water and the speed of the stream.
Sol. Let the speed of the boat in still water be $x \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{hour}$ and the speed of the stream be $y \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{hr}$. Then speed of boat in downstream is $(x+y) \mathrm{km} / \mathrm{hr}$ and the speed of boat in upstream is $(x-y) \mathrm{km} / \mathrm{hr}$.
Case-1 : Distance covered in upstream $=12 \mathrm{~km} \therefore$ time $=\frac{12}{x-y} \mathrm{hr} .\left[\because\right.$ time $\left.=\frac{\text { distance }}{\text { speed }}\right]$
Distance covered in downstream $=40 \mathrm{~km} \quad \therefore$ time $=\frac{40}{x+y} \mathrm{hr}$.
Total time is $8 \mathrm{hrs} . \therefore \frac{12}{x-y}+\frac{40}{x+y}=8$

Case-2 : Distance covered in upstream $=16 \mathrm{~km} \quad \therefore$ time $=\frac{16}{x-y} \mathrm{hr}$., downstream $=32 \mathrm{~km} \quad \therefore$ time $=\frac{32}{x+y} \mathrm{hr}$.
Total time taken $=8$ hrs. $\therefore \frac{16}{x-y}+\frac{32}{x+y}=8$

Let $\frac{1}{x-y}=u \& \frac{1}{x+y}=v$
Substitute this in (i) and (ii) equation.

We get, $12 u+40 v=8$ and $16 u+32 v=8 \Rightarrow 3 u+10 v=2$ and $2 u+4 v=1$

$$
\Rightarrow 6 u+20 v=4
$$

and $6 u+12 v=3$

On subtracting (iv) from (iii), we get

$$
\begin{aligned}
& \begin{array}{l}
6 u+20 v=4 \\
6 u+12 v=13 \\
-\quad-
\end{array} \\
& \hline 8 v=1 \\
& \Rightarrow v=\frac{1}{8}=\frac{1}{x+y} \Rightarrow x+y=8
\end{aligned}
$$

Put value of $v=\frac{1}{8}$ in equation (iv). We get, $6 u+12 \times \frac{1}{8}=3$
$\Rightarrow 12 u+3=6 \Rightarrow u=\frac{1}{4}=\frac{1}{x-y}$
$\Rightarrow x-y=4$

On solving equation (v) and (vi), we get, $x=$ Speed of boat $=6 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{hr}, y=$ speed of stream $=2 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{hr}$.
7. It takes $\mathbf{1 2}$ hours to fill a swimming pool using $\mathbf{2}$ pipes. If the larger pipe is used for $\mathbf{4}$ hours and the smaller pipe for 9 hours, only half the pool is filled. How long would it take for each pipe alone to fill the pool ?
Sol. Let the time taken to fill the pool by the larger pipe $=x$ hours and that by the smaller pipe $=y$ hours.
Therefore, in 1 hour, volume of pool filled by the larger pipe $=1 / x$ and by the smaller pipe $=1 / y$
Given that both pipes can fill the pool in 12 hours.

$$
\therefore \quad \frac{12}{x}+\frac{12}{y}=1
$$

Given also that if the larger pipe is used for 4 hours and the smaller pipe for 9 hours, only half the pool is filled.

$$
\therefore \quad \frac{4}{x}+\frac{9}{y}=\frac{1}{2}
$$

Multiplying equation (ii) by $3, \frac{12}{x}+\frac{27}{y}=\frac{3}{2}$

Subtracting (iii) from (i), $\frac{12}{y}-\frac{27}{y}=1-\frac{3}{2} \Rightarrow-\frac{15}{y}=-\frac{1}{2} \Rightarrow y=30$
Substituting the value of $y$ in equation (i), $\frac{12}{x}+\frac{12}{30}=1 \Rightarrow \frac{12}{x}=1-\frac{12}{30}=1-\frac{2}{5}=\frac{3}{5} \Rightarrow x=20$
Therefore, time taken by the larger pipe $=20 \mathrm{hr}$. and time taken by the smaller pipe $=30 \mathrm{hr}$.
8. A train covered a certain distance at a uniform speed. If the train would have been $\mathbf{1 0 ~ k m} / \mathbf{h}$ faster, it would have taken 2 hours less than the scheduled time. And, if the train were slower by $10 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{h}$; it would have taken 3 hours more than the scheduled time. Find the distance covered by the train.
Sol. Let the initial speed, be $u$, and the distance be $d$; then time, $t=\frac{d}{u}$ or $d=u t$

According to problem : $d=(u+10)(t-2)$ or $u t=u t-2 u+10 t-20$
$\Rightarrow 10 t-2 u=20$

Also $d=(u-10)(t+3)[\because$ decreasing speed by 10 results in increasing of time by 3$]$
$\Rightarrow u t=u t+3 u-10 t-30 \Rightarrow 3 u-10 t=30$

Adding equations (ii) and (iii)

| $-2 u+10 t$ | $=20$ |
| ---: | :--- |
| $3 u-10 t$ | $=30$ |
| $u$ | $=50 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{hr}$ |

Put the value of $u$ in equation (iii)
$\Rightarrow 3 \times 50-10 t=30 \Rightarrow t=12 \mathrm{hrs}$.
From equation (i), the distance covered by the train, $d=u t=50 \times 12=600 \mathrm{~km}$.
9. Solve the following simultaneous equations by using the elimination method:

$$
2 x+3 y=15 ; 4 x-3 y=3
$$

Sol. Label the equations as follows:

$$
2 x+3 y=15 \quad \ldots(\mathrm{i}) \quad 4 x-3 y=3
$$

Note that $3 y$ appears on the left-hand side of both equations. Adding equation (i) and (ii), it gives

$$
2 x+3 y+4 x-3 y=15+3 \Rightarrow 6 x=18 \therefore x=\frac{18}{6} \Rightarrow x=3
$$

Substituting $x=3$ in (i), gives :

$$
2 \times 3+3 y=15 \Rightarrow 6+3 y=15 \Rightarrow 3 y=15-6 \Rightarrow 3 y=9 \Rightarrow y=\frac{9}{3} \Rightarrow y=3
$$

So, the solution is $x=3, y=3$
10. A vessel contains mixture of $24 l$ milk and $6 l$ water and a second vessel contains a mixture of $15 l$ milk and $10 l$ water. How much mixture of milk and water should be taken from the first and the second vessel separately and kept in a third vessel so that the third vessel may contain a mixture of $25 l$ milk and $10 l$ water?
Sol. Let $x l$ of mixture be taken from $1^{\text {st }}$ vessel and $y l$ of the mixture be taken from $2^{\text {nd }}$ vessel and kept in $3^{\text {rd }}$ vessel so that $(x+y) l$ of the mixture in third vessel may contain $25 l$ of milk and $10 l$ of water.
A mixture of $x l$ from $1^{\text {st }}$ vessel contains $\frac{24}{30} x=\frac{4}{5} x l$ of milk and $\frac{x}{5} l$ of water. And a mixture of $y l$ from $2^{\text {nd }}$ vessel contains $\frac{3 y}{5} l$ of milk and $\frac{2 y}{5} l$ of water.

$$
\begin{aligned}
\therefore \quad \frac{4}{5} x+\frac{3}{5} y & =25 \\
\frac{x}{5}+\frac{2}{5} y & =10
\end{aligned}
$$

On multiply equation (ii) by 4 , we get, $\frac{4}{5} x+\frac{8}{5} y=40$

Subtracting equation (i) from (iii), we get, $\frac{4}{5} x-\frac{4}{5} x+\frac{8}{5} y-\frac{3}{5} y=40-25 \Rightarrow y=15$
On putting the value of $y$ in equation (i), $\frac{4}{5} x+\frac{3}{5} \times 15=25 \Rightarrow \frac{4}{5} x+9=25 \Rightarrow x=20$
11. Solve the systems of equations graphically :

$$
\begin{aligned}
& 2 x+3 y=10 \\
& 3 x-y=4
\end{aligned}
$$

Sol.

$$
\begin{array}{ll}
2 x+3 y=10 & 3 x-y=4 \\
\Rightarrow 3 y=10-2 x & \Rightarrow-y=4-3 x \\
\Rightarrow y=\frac{10-2 x}{3} & \Rightarrow y=3 x-4
\end{array}
$$

When $x=5, y=\frac{10-2(5)}{3}=0$
When $x=0, y=3$
(0) $-4=-4$

When $x=8, y=\frac{10-2(8)}{3}=-2 \quad$ When $x=1, y=3$
(1) $-4=-1$

When $x=11, y=\frac{10-2(11)}{3}=-4$ When $x=3, y=3$
(3) $-4=5$

Table for $2 x+3 y=10$ is
Table for $3 x-y=4$ is

| $\boldsymbol{x}$ | 5 | 8 | 11 |
| :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: |
| $\boldsymbol{y}$ | 0 | -2 | -4 |


| $\boldsymbol{x}$ | 0 | 1 | 3 |
| :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: |
| $\boldsymbol{y}$ | -4 | -1 | 5 |

![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-072.jpg?height=682&width=679&top_left_y=1628&top_left_x=1135)

From the graph, the solution is the point of intersection of the lines, i.e., $x=2, y=2$.
12. Solve the system of equations by the cross-multiplication method.
$\boldsymbol{a} \boldsymbol{x} \boldsymbol{+} \boldsymbol{b} \boldsymbol{y} \boldsymbol{=} \boldsymbol{c} \boldsymbol{;}$

$$
b x-a y=c
$$

Sol. $a x+b y=c, b x-a y=c \Rightarrow a x+b y-c=0, b x-a y-c=0$
Using the cross-multiplication method,

$$
\begin{aligned}
& \frac{x}{-b c-a c}=\frac{y}{-b c+a c}=\frac{1}{-a^{2}-b^{2}} \\
\Rightarrow & x=\frac{-a c-b c}{-a^{2}-b^{2}}=\frac{-c(a+b)}{-\left(a^{2}+b^{2}\right)}=\frac{c(a+b)}{a^{2}+b^{2}} \text { and } y=\frac{a c-b c}{-a^{2}-b^{2}}=\frac{c(a-b)}{-\left(a^{2}+b^{2}\right)}=-\frac{c(a-b)}{a^{2}+b^{2}}
\end{aligned}
$$

Therefore, $x=\frac{c(a+b)}{a^{2}+b^{2}}, y=-\frac{c(a-b)}{a^{2}+b^{2}}$
13. Solve the system of equations :

$$
a x+b y=1, b x+a y=\frac{2 a b}{a^{2}+b^{2}}
$$

Sol. $a x+b y=1$

$$
b x+a y=\frac{2 a b}{a^{2}+b^{2}}
$$

On adding (i) and (ii), we get

$$
\begin{aligned}
& (a+b) x+(a+b) y=1+\frac{2 a b}{a^{2}+b^{2}} ;(a+b) x+(a+b) y=\frac{a^{2}+b^{2}+2 a b}{a^{2}+b^{2}} \\
\Rightarrow & (a+b)(x+y)=\frac{(a+b)^{2}}{a^{2}+b^{2}} \Rightarrow x+y=\frac{a+b}{a^{2}+b^{2}}
\end{aligned}
$$

Subtracting equation (ii) from (i)

$$
\begin{aligned}
& (a-b) x+(b-a) y=1-\frac{2 a b}{a^{2}+b^{2}} \Rightarrow(a-b) x-(a-b) y=\frac{a^{2}+b^{2}-2 a b}{a^{2}+b^{2}} \\
\Rightarrow & (a-b)(x-y)=\frac{(a-b)^{2}}{a^{2}+b^{2}} \Rightarrow x-y=\frac{a-b}{a^{2}+b^{2}}
\end{aligned}
$$

Adding (iii) and (iv),

$$
2 x=\frac{a+b}{a^{2}+b^{2}}+\frac{a-b}{a^{2}+b^{2}}=\frac{2 a}{a^{2}+b^{2}} \Rightarrow x=\frac{a}{a^{2}+b^{2}}
$$

Subtracting (iv) from (iii)

$$
2 y=\frac{a+b}{a^{2}+b^{2}}-\frac{a-b}{a^{2}+b^{2}}=\frac{2 b}{a^{2}+b^{2}} \Rightarrow y=\frac{b}{a^{2}+b^{2}}
$$

Therefore the solution is $x=\frac{a}{a^{2}+b^{2}}, y=\frac{b}{a^{2}+b^{2}}$
Alternate way

## Alternate way

$$
\begin{aligned}
& a x+b y=1 \\
& b x+a y=\frac{2 a b}{a^{2}+b^{2}}
\end{aligned}
$$

Multiplying (i) by $a x \&$ (ii) by $x b$
We get, $a^{2} x+a b y=a$
and $b^{2} x+a b y=\frac{2 a b^{2}}{a^{2}+b^{2}}$

On subtracting (iv) from (iii), we get,
$x\left(a^{2}-b^{2}\right)=a-\frac{2 a b^{2}}{a^{2}+b^{2}}=\frac{a^{3}-a b^{2}}{a^{2}+b^{2}}=\frac{a\left(a^{2}-b^{2}\right)}{a^{2}+b^{2}} \Rightarrow x=\frac{a}{a^{2}+b^{2}}$
Put value of $x$ in equation (i), we get,
$a \times \frac{a}{a^{2}+b^{2}}+b y=1 \Rightarrow b y=\frac{b^{2}}{a^{2}+b^{2}} \Rightarrow y=\frac{b}{a^{2}+b^{2}}$

## Multiple Choice Questions

DIRECTIONS : This section contains multiple choice questions. Each question has 4 choices (a), (b), (c) and (d) out of which only one is correct.

1. For which value of $p$, will the lines represented by the following pair of linear equations be parallel
$3 x-y-5=0$
$6 x-2 y-p=0$
(a) all real values except 10
(b) 10
(c) $5 / 2$
(d) $1 / 2$
2. For what values of $k$ will the following pair of linear equations have infinitely many solutions?
$k x+3 y-(k-3)=0$
$12 x+k y-k=0$
(a) $k=4$
(b) $\quad k=3$
(c) $k=6$
(d) $k=2$
3. Which of the following is true if following pair of linear equations has unique solution?
$3 x-2 y=-8$
$(2 m-5) x+7 y-6=0$
(a) $m=\frac{11}{4}$
(b) $m=-\frac{11}{4}$
(c) $m \neq-\frac{11}{4}$
(d) $\quad m \neq \frac{11}{4}$
4. The perimeter of a rectangle is 40 cm . The ratio of its sides is $2: 3$. Find its length and breadth.
(a) $l=10 \mathrm{~cm}, \mathrm{~b}=8 \mathrm{~cm}$
(b) $l=12 \mathrm{~cm}, \mathrm{~b}=8 \mathrm{~cm}$
(c) $l=12 \mathrm{~m}, \mathrm{~b}=8 \mathrm{~m}$
(d) $l=40 \mathrm{~m}, \mathrm{~b}=30 \mathrm{~m}$
5. Which of the following is the other name for a pair of linear equations in two variables?
(a) Consistent equations
(b) Simultaneous equations
(c) Inconsistent equations
(d) Dependent equations
6. The difference between two numbers is 26 and one number is three times the other. Find them.
(a) 39,13
(b)
41,67
(c) 96, 70
(d) 52,26
7. Sanjay starts his job with a certain monthly salary and earns a fixed increment every year. If his salary was ₹ 4500 after four years of service and ₹ 5400 after 10 years, find his initial salary and annual increment.
(a) 4000,200
(b) 3900,150
(c) 4500,100
(d) 3800,250
8. The pair of linear equations $x+2 y=5$ and $3 x+12 y=10$ has
(a) unique solution
(b) no solution
(c) more than two solutions
(d) infinitely many solutions
9. ₹ 49 was divided among 150 children. Each girl got 50 paise and each boy got 25 paise. How many boys were there?
(a) 100
(b) 102
(c) 104
(d) 105
10. If the sum of the ages (in years) of a father and his son is 65 and twice the difference of their ages (in years) is 50, what is the age of the father?
(a) 45 years
(b) 40 years
(c) 50 years
(d) 55 years
11. The value of $k$ for which the system of linear equations $x+2 y=3,5 x+k y+7=0$ is inconsistent is
(a) $-\frac{14}{3}$
(b) $\frac{2}{5}$
(c) 5
(d) 10

## Fill in the Blanks

## DIRECTIONS : Complete the following statements with an appropriate word / term to be filled in the blank space(s).

1. If the lines intersect at a point, then that point gives the unique solution of the two equations. In this case, the pair of equations is $\_\_\_\_$
2. If the lines are parallel, then the pair of equations has no solution. In this case, the pair of equations is $\_\_\_\_$
3. Two distinct natural numbers are such that the sum of one number and twice the other number is 6 . The two numbers are $\_\_\_\_$
4. If $p+q=k, p-q=n$ and $k>n$, then $q$ is $\_\_\_\_$ (positive/negative).
5. Sum of the ages of $X$ and $Y, 12$ years, ago, was 48 years and sum of the ages of $X$ and $Y$, 12 years hence will be 96 years. Present age of $X$ is $\_\_\_\_$
6. The number of common solutions for the system of linear equations $5 x+4 y+6=0$ and $10 x+8 y=12$ is $\_\_\_\_$
7. If $2 x+3 y=5$ and $3 x+2 y=10$, then $x-y=$ $\_\_\_\_$ •
8. If $\frac{1}{x}+\frac{1}{y}=k$ and $\frac{1}{x}-\frac{1}{y}=k$, then the value of $y$ is $\_\_\_\_$

## True / False

## DIRECTIONS : Read the following statements and write your answer as true or false.

1. If a pair of linear equations is given by $a_{1} x+b_{1} y+c_{1}=0$ and $a_{2} x+b_{2} y+c_{2}=0$ and $\frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}} \neq \frac{b_{1}}{b_{2}}$. In this case, the pair of linear equations is consistent.
2. If a pair of linear equations is given by $a_{1} x+b_{1} y+c_{1}=0$ and $a_{2} x+b_{2} y+c_{2}=0$ and $\frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}}=\frac{b_{1}}{b_{2}} \neq \frac{c_{1}}{c_{2}}$. In this case, the pair of linear equations is consistent.
3. If a pair of linear equations is given by $a_{1} x+b_{1} y+c_{1}=0$ and $a_{2} x+b_{2} y+c_{2}=0$ and $\frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}}=\frac{b_{1}}{b_{2}}=\frac{c_{1}}{c_{2}}$. In this case, the pair of linear equations is consistent.
4. $3 x-y=3,9 x-3 y=9$ has infinite solution.
5. $\sqrt{2} x+\sqrt{3} y=0, \sqrt{3} x-\sqrt{8} y=0$ has no solution.
6. $3 x+2 y=5,2 x-3 y=7$ are consistent pair of equation.
7. In a $\triangle \mathrm{ABC}, \angle \mathrm{C}=3 \angle \mathrm{~B}=2(\angle \mathrm{~A}+\angle \mathrm{B})$, then angles are $20^{\circ}, 40^{\circ}, 100^{\circ}$.

## Match the Following

DIRECTIONS : Each question contains statements given in two columns which have to be matched. Statements (A, B, C, D) in column-I have to be matched with statements ( $p, q, r, s$ ) in

## Column-I

(A) $2 x+y=8, x+6 y=15$
(B) $5 x+3 y=35,2 x+4 y=28$
(C) $\frac{1}{7 x}+\frac{1}{6 y}=3, \frac{1}{2 x}-\frac{1}{3 y}=5$
(D) $15 x+4 y=61 4 x+15 y=72$ column-II.

1. Column-II give value of $x$ and $y$ for pair of equation given in Column-I.

## Column-II

(p) $(3,4)$
(q) $(1 / 14,1 / 6)$
(r) $(4,5)$
(s) $(3,2)$

Column-II
(p) Infinite solutions
(q) Consistent
(r) No solution
(s) Inconsistent

## Very Short Answer Questions

1. Solve the equations

$$
\begin{aligned}
& 3 x+2 y=11 \\
& 2 x+3 y=4
\end{aligned}
$$

2. Check whether the following given pair of equations has no solution, unique solution or infinite solutions.

$$
\begin{aligned}
& 3 x+4 y=8 \\
& 9 x+12 y=24
\end{aligned}
$$

3. For what value of $k$ will the equations $x+5 y-7=0$ and $4 x+20 y+k=0$ represent coincident lines?
4. If $f(x)=a x+b$, then, find the zero of $f(x)$
5. Solve the following pair of linear equations by substitution method:

$$
\begin{aligned}
& 3 x+2 y-7=0 \\
& 4 x+y-6=0
\end{aligned}
$$

6. Find whether the lines represented by $2 x+y=3$ and $4 x+2 y=6$ are parallel, coincident or intersecting.
7. In Fig., ABCD is rectangle. Find the values of $x$ and $y$.
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-075.jpg?height=268&width=503&top_left_y=2293&top_left_x=402)
8. Find $c$ if the system of equations
$c x+3 y+(3-c)=0 ; 12 x+c y-c=0$ has infinitely many solutions?

## Short Answer Questions

1. Find the values of $x$ and $y$ in the system of equations.

$$
\begin{aligned}
& a x+b y-a+b=0 \\
& b x-a y-a-b=0
\end{aligned}
$$

2. Determine the values of $a$ and $b$ for which the following system of linear equations has infinitely many solutions:

$$
3 x-(a+1) y=2 b-1, \quad 5 x+(1-2 a) y=3 b
$$

3. Find the value of $a$ and $b$ so that the following system of equations have infinitely many solution.
$2 x-y=5 ;(a-2 b) x-(a+b) y=15$
4. Solve the following system of linear equations :

$$
\begin{aligned}
& 2(a x-b y)+(a+4 b)=0 \\
& 2(b x+a y)+(b-4 a)=0
\end{aligned}
$$

5. Draw the graph of $x-y+1=0$ and $2 x+y-10=0$. Calculate the area bounded by these lines and $x$-axis.
6. For what value of $p$ will the following system of equations represent coincident lines?

$$
\begin{aligned}
& x+5 y=7 \\
& 3 x+15 y=p
\end{aligned}
$$

7. Determine the value of $k$ for which the following system of equations becomes consistent:
$7 x-y=5,21 x-3 y=k$.
8. One hundred men in 10 days do one third of a piece of work. The work is then required to be completed in another 13 days. On the next day (the eleventh day) 50 more men are employed, and on the day after that, another 50. How many men must be relieved at the end of the 18th day so that the rest of the men, working for the remaining time, will just complete the work?
9. Points $A$ and $B$ are 90 km apart from each other on a highway. A car starts from $A$ and another from $B$ at the same time. If they go in the same direction, they meet in 9 hrs . and if they go in opposite directions, they meet in $9 / 7 \mathrm{hrs}$. Find their speeds.
10. A lady has 25 p and 50 p coins in her purse. If in all she has 40 coins totalling ₹ 12.50 , find the number of coins of each type she has.
11. The monthly incomes of $A$ and $B$ are in the ratio of $9: 7$ and their monthly expenditures are in the ratio of $4: 3$. If each saves ₹ 1600 per month, find the monthly income of each.
12. Find the value of $P$ for which the given system of equations has only one solution (i.e., unique solution)
$P x-y=2 ; 6 x-2 y=3$
13. Sanjay starts his job with a certain monthly salary and earns a fixed increment every year. If his salary was ₹ 4500 after four years of service and ₹ 5400 after 10 years, find his initial salary and annual increment.
14. Ramesh travels 760 km to his home partly by train and partly by car. He takes 8 hr , if he travels 160 km by train and the rest by car. He takes 12 minutes more, if he travels 240 km by train and the rest by car. Find the speed of train and the car.
15. The perimeter of a rectangle is 40 cm . The ratio of its side is $2: 3$. Find its length and breadth.
16. Solve the equations

$$
\begin{aligned}
& \frac{x}{3}+\frac{y}{2}=4 \\
& \frac{2 x}{3}-\frac{y}{4}=3
\end{aligned}
$$

17. Determine the value of $c$ for which the following system of linear equations has no solution:

$$
c x+3 y=3, \quad 12 x+c y=6
$$

18. Sum of the ages of a father and the son is 40 years. If father's age is three times that of his son, then find their respective ages.]
19. A part of monthly hostel charge is fixed and the remaining depends on the number of days one has taken food in the mess. When Swati takes food for 20 days, she has to pay ₹ 3,000 as hostel charges whereas Mansi who takes food for 25 days $₹ 3,500$ as hostel charges. Find the fixed charges and the cost of food per day.
20 A fraction becomes $1 / 3$ when 2 is subtracted from the numerator and it becomes $1 / 2$ when 1 is subtracted from the denominator. Find the fraction.
20. If $2 x+y=23$ and $4 x-y=19$, find the value of $(5 y-2 x)$ and $\left(\frac{y}{x}-2\right)$.

## Long Answer Questions

1. A man travels 600 km partly by train and partly by car. If he covers 400 km by train and the rest by car, it takes him 6 hours and 30 minutes. But if he travels 200 km by train and rest by car, he takes half an hour longer. Find the speed of the train and that of the car.
2. Solve the following system of linear equations graphically: $x-y=1,2 x+y=8$.
Shade the area bounded by these two lines and the Y-axis.
3. 4 chairs and 3 tables cost $₹ 2100$ and 5 chairs and 2 tables cost ₹1750. Find the cost of one chair and one table separately.
4. For Uttarakhand flood victims two sections A and B of class X contributed 1,500 . If the contribution X -A was 100 less than that of X-B, find graphically the amounts contributed by both the sections.
5. It can take 12 hours to fill a swimming pool using two pipes. If the pipe of larger diameter is used for four hours and the pipe of smaller diameter for 9 hours, only half of the pool can be filled. How long would it take for each pipe to fill the pool separately?

## Reasoning Based Questions

1. Do the following pair of linear equations have no solution? Justify your answer.
(i) $5 x+6 y=30$
(ii) $x=2 y$
$10 x+12 y=40$
$y=2 x$
(iii) $27 x+3 y=7$

$$
3 x+\frac{9}{2} y=12
$$

2. Are the following pair of linear equations consistent? Justify your answer.
(i) $-2 x-3 y=1$
(ii) $2 a x+b y=a$
$6 x+4 x=-2$
$4 a x+2 b y-2 a=0$; $a, b \neq 0$
3. For pair of equations
$2 x+5 y=k \quad k x+15 y=18$
to have infinitely many solutions the value of $k$ should be 6. Is the statement true? Give reason.
4. The line represented by $x=7$ is parallel to $x$-axis. Justify whether the statement is true or false.
5. The sum of numerator \& denominator of a fraction is 8 .

If 3 is added to both the fraction becomes $\frac{3}{4}$. Justify whether the original fraction is $\frac{5}{3}$ or not.

## HOTS Questions

1. A two digit number is obtained by either multiplying sum of the digits by 8 and adding 1 or by multiplying the difference of the digits by 13 and adding 2 . Find the number.
2. The sum of a two digit number and the number obtained by reversing the order of its digits is 99 . If the digits differ by 3 , find the number.
3. Solve the following pairs of equations by reducing them to a pair of linear equations:
$\frac{1}{3 x+y}+\frac{1}{3 x-y}=\frac{3}{4} ; \frac{1}{2(3 x+y)}-\frac{1}{2(3 x-y)}=\frac{-1}{8}$

## Text-book Questions

1. Yash scored 40 marks in a test, getting 3 marks for each right answer and losing 1 mark for each wrong answer. If 4 marks had been awarded for each correct answer and 2 marks had been deducted for each incorrect answer, then Yash would have scored 50 marks. How many questions were there in the test?
2. Aftab tells his daughter, "Seven years ago, I was seven times as old as you were then. Also, three years from now, I shall be three times as old as you will be." (Isn' this interesting?) Represent this situation algebraically and graphically.
3. Half the perimeter of a rectangular garden, whose length is 4 m more than its width, is 36 m . Find the dimensions of the garden.
4. Solve $2 x+3 y=11$ and $2 x-4 y=-24$ and hence find the value of ' $m$ ' for which $y=m x+3$.
5. Form the pair of linear equations in the following problems, and find their solutions (if they exist) by the elimination method :
(i) Five years ago, Nuri was thrice as old as Sonu. Ten years later, Nuri will be twice as old as Sonu. How old are Nuri and Sonu?
(ii) A lending library has a fixed charge for the first three days and an additional charge for each day thereafter. Saritha paid ₹ 27 for a book kept for seven days, while Susy paid ₹ 21 for the book she kept for five days. Find the fixed charge and the charge for each extra day.
6. Check whether the given pair of linear equations has unique solution, no solution, or infinitely many solutions. In case there is a unique solution, find it by using cross multiplication method.
$3 x-5 y=20$
$6 x-10 y=40$
7. Form the pair of linear equations in the following problem and find their solutions (if they exist) by any algebraic method :
The area of a rectangle gets reduced by 9 square units, if its length is reduced by 5 units and breadth is increased by 3 units. If we increase the length by 3 units and the breadth by 2 units, the area increases by 67 square units. Find the dimensions of the rectangle.
8. Solve the following pairs of equations by reducing them to a pair of linear equations:

$$
\begin{aligned}
& 6 x+3 y=6 x y \\
& 2 x+4 y=5 x y
\end{aligned}
$$

9. One says, "Give me a hundered, friend! I shall then become twice as rich as you". The other replies, "If you given me ten, I shall be six times as rich as you". Tell me what is the amount of their (respective) capital?
[Hint : $x+100=2(y-100), y+10=6(x-10)]$.
10. Solve the following pair of linear equations:
$152 x-378 y=-74$
$-378 x+152 y=-604$
11. Aruna has only ₹ 1 and ₹ 2 coins with her. If the total number of coins that she has is 50 and the amount of money with her is ₹ 75 , then the number of ₹ 1 and ₹ 2 coins are, respectively
(a) 35 and 15
(b) 35 and 20
(c) 15 and 35
(d) 25 and 25
12. For all real values of $c$, the pair of equations
$x-2 y=8$
$5 x-10 y=c$
have a unique solution. Justify whether it is true or false.
13. Find the solution of the pair of equations

$$
\frac{x}{10}+\frac{y}{5}-1=0 \text { and } \frac{x}{8}+\frac{y}{6}=15
$$

Hence, find $\lambda$, if $y=\lambda x+5$.
14. The cost of 4 pens and 4 pencil boxes is $₹ 100$. Three times the cost of a pen is ₹ 15 more than the cost of a pencil box. Form the pair of linear equations for the above situation. Find the cost of a pen and a pencil box.

## Exemplar Questions

1. The pair of equations $5 x-15 y=8$ and $3 x-9 y=\frac{24}{5}$ has
(a) one solution
(b) two solutions
(c) infinitely many solutions
(d) no solution
2. The sum of the digits of a two-digit number is 9 . If 27 is added to it, the digits of the number get reversed. The number is
(a) 25
(b) 72
(c) 63
(d) 36
3. The value of $c$ for which the pair of equations $c x-y=2$ and $6 x+2 y=3$ will have infinitely many solutions is
(a) 3
(b) -3
(c) -12
(d) no value
4. If $x=a, y=b$ is the solution of the equations $x-y=2$ and $x+y=4$, then the values of $a$ and $b$ are, respectively.
(a) 3 and 5
(b) 5 and 3
(c) 3 and 1
(d) - 1 and - 3

## Exercise 3

## Foundation Builder

## Single Option Correct

DIRECTIONS : This section contains multiple choice questions. Each question has 4 choices (a), (b), (c) and (d) out of which ONLY ONE is correct.

1. $A$ can do a piece of work in 24 days. If $B$ is $60 \%$ more efficient than $A$, then the number of days required by $B$ to do the twice as large as the earlier work is
(a) 24
(b) 36
(c) 15
(d) 30
2. X's salary is half that of Y's. If $X$ got a $50 \%$ rise in his salary and $Y$ got $25 \%$ rise in his salary, then the percentage increase in combined salaries of both is
(a) 30
(b) $33 \frac{1}{3}$
(c) $37 \frac{1}{2}$
(d) 75
3. The points $(7,2)$ and $(-1,0)$ lie on a line
(a) $7 y=3 x-7$
(b) $4 y=x+1$
(c) $y=7 x+7$
(d) $x=4 y+1$
4. At present ages of a father and his son are in the ratio $7: 3$, and they will be in the ratio $2: 1$ after 10 years. Then the present age of father (in years) is
(a) 42
(b) 56
(c) 70
(d) 77
5. A fraction becomes 4 when 1 is added to both the numerator and denominator and it becomes 7 when 1 is subtracted from both the numerator and denominator. The numerator of the given fraction is
(a) 2
(b) 3
(c) 5
(d) 15
6. A motor boat takes 2 hours to travel a distance 9 km down the current and it takes 6 hours to travel the same distance against the current. The speed of the boat in still water and that of the current (in km/hour) respectively are
(a) $3,1.5$
(b) 3,2
(c) $3.5,2.5$
(d) 3,1
7. The 2 digit number which becomes (5/6)th of itself when its digits are reversed. The difference in the digits of the number being 1 , then the two digits number is
(a) 45
(b) 54
(c) 36
(d) None of these
8. $x$ and $y$ are 2 different digits. If the sum of the two digit numbers formed by using both the digits is a perfect square, then value of $x+y$ is
(a) 10
(b) 11
(c) 12
(d) 13
9. If $3 x+4 y: x+2 y=9: 4$, then $3 x+5 y: 3 x-y$ is equal to
(a) $4: 1$
(b) $1: 4$
(c) $7: 1$
(d) $1: 7$
10. In a number of two digits, unit's digit is twice the tens digit. If 36 be added to the number, the digits are reversed. The number is
(a) 36
(b) 63
(c) 48
(d) 84
11. A man can row a boat in still water at the rate of 6 km per hour. If the stream flows at the rate of $2 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{hr}$, he takes half the time going downstream than going upstream the same distance. His average speed for upstream and down stream trip is
(a) $6 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{hr}$
(b) $16 / 3 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{hr}$
(c) Insufficient data to arrive at the answer
(d) none of the above
12. A boat travels with a speed of $15 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{hr}$ in still water. In a river flowing at $5 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{hr}$, the boat travels some distance downstream and then returns. The ratio of average speed to the speed in still water is
(a) $8: 3$
(b) $3: 8$
(c) $8: 9$
(d) $9: 8$
13. $x$ and $y$ are two non-negative numbers such that $2 x+y=10$. The sum of the maximum and minimum values of $(x+y)$ is
[NTSE]
(a) 6
(b) 9
(c) 10
(d) 15
14. The average incomes of the people in two villages are $P$ and $Q$ respectively. Assume that $P \neq Q . A$ person moves from the first village to the second village. The new average incomes are $P^{\prime}$ and $Q^{\prime}$ respectively. Which of the following is not possible?
[KVPY]
(a) $P^{\prime}>P$ and $Q^{\prime}>Q$
(b) $P^{\prime}>P$ and $Q^{\prime}<Q$
(c) $P^{\prime}=P$ and $Q^{\prime}=Q$
(d) $P^{\prime}<P$ and $Q^{\prime}<Q$
15. The graphs of the equations $x-y=2$ and $k x+y=3$, where $k$ is a constant, intersect at the point $(x, y)$ in the first quadrant, if and only if $k$ is
[NTSE]
(a) equal to -1
(b) greater than -1
(c) less than $3 / 2$
(d) lying between- 1 and $3 / 2$
16. For what value of $p$, the following pair of linear equations in two variables will have infinitely many solutions?
$p x+3 y-(p-3)=0,12 x+p y-p=0$
[NTSE]
(a) 6
(b) -6
(c) 0
(d) 2
17. In a classroom, one-fifth of the boys leave the class and the ratio of the remaining boys to girls is $2: 3$. If further 44 girls leave the class, then the ratio of boys to girls is 5: 2. How many more boys should leave the class so that the number of boys equals that of girls?
[KVPY]
(a) 16
(b) 24
(c) 30
(d) 36
18. The equations $\frac{1}{x}+\frac{1}{y}=15$ and $\frac{1}{x}-\frac{1}{y}=5$ are such that $a x=1$ and $b y=1$. The values of ' $a$ ' and ' $b$ ' respectively are
[JSTSE]
(a) 10,5
(b) $10,-5$
(c) $-5,10$
(d) 5,10
19. Consider the following two statements:
I. Any pair of consistent linear equations in two variables must have a unique solution.
II. There do not exist two consecutive integers, the sum of whose squares is 365 .
[KVPY]
Then,
(a) both I and II are true
(b) both I and II are false
(c) I is true and II is false
(d) I is false and II is true
20. In village Madhubani 8 women and 12 girls can paint a large mural in 10 hours. 6 women and 8 girls can paint it in 14 hours. The number of hours taken by 7 women and 14 girls to paint the mural is
[NTSE]
(a) 10
(b) 15
(c) 20
(d) 35
21. A boat takes 3 hours to travel 30 km downstream and takes 5 hours to return to the same spot upstream. Find the speed of the boat in still water. ( $\mathrm{km} / \mathrm{hr}$ )
[NTSE]
(a) $10 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{hr}$
(b) $8 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{hr}$
(c) $6 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{hr}$
(d) $5 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{hr}$

## More Than One Option Correct

DIRECTIONS : This section contains multiple choice questions. Each question has 4 choices (a), (b), (c) and (d) out of which ONE OR MORE may be correct.

1. I. If $x-y=x y=1-x-y$, then $x+y$ is $\frac{5}{6}$
II. The system of equations $3 x+2 y=a$ and $5 x+b y =4$ has infinitely many solutions for $x$ and $y$, then $a=4, b=3$
III. If $\frac{x}{a}+\frac{y}{b}=2$ and $a x-b y=a^{2}-b^{2}$, then $x=a, y=b$

Which is true?
(a) I only
(b) II only
(c) III only
(d) None of these.
2. I. If $3 x-5 y=-1$ and $x-y=-1$, then $x=-2, y=-1$
II. $2 x+3 y=9,3 x+4 y=5 \Rightarrow x=-21, y=17$
III. $\frac{2 x}{a}+\frac{y}{b}=2, \frac{x}{a}-\frac{y}{b}=4 \Rightarrow x=2 a, y=-2 b$

Which is true?
(a) I
(b) II
(c) III
(d) None of these
3. If a pair of linear equations is consistent, then the lines will be
(a) parallel
(b) always coincident
(c) intersecting
(d) coincident
4. For what values of $k$, do the equations $3 x-y+8=0$ and $6 x-k y=-16$ represent coincident lines?
(a) solution of $3 k-9=0$
(b) solution of $2 k-8=0$
(c) 2
(d) 3

## Passage Based Questions

DIRECTIONS: Study the given paragraph(s) and answer the following questions.

## Passage-I

If we have two simultaneous equations

$$
a x+b y=c
$$

and $\quad b x+a y=d$, then in order to solve
we find (i) + (ii) and then (i) - (ii), we shall get

$$
\begin{array}{ll} 
& (a+b) x+(a+b) y=c+d \\
\text { i.e. } & x+y=\frac{c+d}{a+b} \\
\text { and } & (a-b) x-(a-b) y=c-d \\
\text { i.e. } & x-y=\frac{c-d}{a-b}
\end{array}
$$

To find $x$, (iii) + (iv) gives, $2 x=\frac{c+d}{a+b}+\frac{c-d}{a-b}$
$\Rightarrow \quad x=\frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{c+d}{a+b}+\frac{c-d}{a-b}\right)$
To find $y$, (iii) + (iv) gives, $y=\frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{c+d}{a+b}-\frac{c-d}{a-b}\right)$

1. The solution of

$$
\begin{aligned}
& 217 x+131 y=913 \\
& 131 x+217 y=827 \text { is }
\end{aligned}
$$

(a) $x=2, y=3$
(b) $x=3, y=2$
(c) $x=2, y=2$
(d) $x=3, y=3$
2. The solution of

$$
\begin{aligned}
& 37 x+41 y=70 \\
& 41 x+37 y=86 \text { is }
\end{aligned}
$$

(a) $x=3, y=1$
(b) $x=3, y=-1$
(c) $x=-3, y=1$
(d) $x=1, y=3$
3. The solution of

$$
\begin{aligned}
& x+2 y=\frac{3}{2} \\
& 2 x+y=\frac{3}{2} \text { is }
\end{aligned}
$$

(a) $x=3, y=1$
(b) $x=\frac{1}{2}, y=\frac{1}{2}$
(c) $x=\frac{1}{2}, y=0$
(d) $x=0, y=\frac{1}{2}$

## Passage -II

A system of linear equations is given as follows:
$a_{1} x+b_{1} y+c_{1}=0$
$a_{2} x+b_{2} y+c_{2}=0$
4. Condition for two lines to have a unique solution is
(a) $\frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}}=\frac{b_{1}}{b_{2}}$
(b) $\frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}} \neq \frac{c_{1}}{c_{2}}$
(c) $\frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}}=\frac{b_{1}}{b_{2}} \neq \frac{c_{1}}{c_{2}}$
(d) $\frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}}=\frac{b_{1}}{b_{2}}=\frac{c_{1}}{c_{2}}$
5. Condition for two lines to have infinitely many solutions is
(a) $\frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}}=\frac{b_{1}}{b_{2}}=\frac{c_{1}}{c_{2}}$
(b) $\frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}} \neq \frac{c_{1}}{c_{2}}$
(c) $\frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}}=\frac{b_{1}}{b_{2}} \neq \frac{c_{1}}{c_{2}}$
(d) None of these
6. Both lines are parallel only if
(a) $\frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}}=\frac{b_{1}}{b_{2}}$
(b) $\frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}}=\frac{b_{1}}{b_{2}} \neq \frac{c_{1}}{c_{2}}$
(c) $\frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}}=\frac{b_{1}}{b_{2}}=\frac{c_{1}}{c_{2}}$
(d) None of these

## Assertion \& Reason

DIRECTIONS : Each of these questions contains an Assertion followed by Reason. Read them carefully and answer the question on the basis of following options. You have to select the one that best describes the two statements.
(a) If both Assertion and Reason are correct and Reason is the correct explanation of Assertion.
(b) If both Assertion and Reason are correct, but Reason is not the correct explanation of Assertion.
(c) If Assertion is correct but Reason is incorrect.
(d) If Assertion is incorrect but Reason is correct.

1. Assertion : $3 x+4 y+5=0$ and $6 x+k y+9=0$ represent parallel lines if $k=8$
Reason : $a_{1} x+b_{1} y+c_{1}=0$ and $a_{2} x+b_{2} y+c_{2}=0$ represent parallel lines if $\frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}}=\frac{b_{1}}{b_{2}} \neq \frac{c_{1}}{c_{2}}$
2. Assertion : $x+y-4=0$ and $2 x+k y-3=0$ has no solution if $k=2$
Reason : $a_{1} x+b_{1} y+c_{1}=0$ and $a_{2} x+b_{2} y+c_{2}=0$ are consistent if $\frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}} \neq \frac{b_{1}}{b_{2}}$
3. Assertion : If the system of equations $2 x+3 y=7$ and $2 a x+(a+b) y=28$ has infinitely many solutions, then $2 a-b=0$
Reason : The system of equations $3 x-5 y=9$ and $6 x-10 y=8$ has a unique solution.
4. Assertion : If the pair of lines are coincident, then we say that pair of lines is consistent and it has a unique solution. Reason : If the pair of lines are parallel, then the pair has no solution and is called inconsistent pair of equations.
5. Assertion : If $k x-y-2=0$ and $6 x-2 y-3=0$ are inconsistent, then $k=3$
Reason : $a_{1} x+b_{1} y+c_{1}=0$ and $a_{2} x+b_{2} y+c_{2}=0$ are inconsistent if $\frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}}=\frac{b_{1}}{b_{2}} \neq \frac{c_{1}}{c_{2}}$
6. Assertion : $3 x-4 y=7$ and $6 x-8 y=k$ have infinite number of solution if $k=14$
Reason : $a_{1} x+b_{1} y+c_{1}=0$ and $a_{2} x+b_{2} y+c_{2}=0$ have a unique solution if $\frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}} \neq \frac{b_{1}}{b_{2}}$
7. Column-I
(A) No solution
(B) Infinitely many solutions
(C) Unique solution
(D) System is consistent

## Column-II

(p) $5 x-15 y=8,3 x-9 y=\frac{24}{5}$
(q) $2 x+4 y=10,3 x+6 y=12$
(r) $3 x-2 y=4,6 x-4 y=8$
(s) $2 x+y=6,4 x-2 y-4=0$
(t) $3 x-y=8, x-\frac{y}{3}=3$
(u) $x-y=8,3 x-3 y=16$
7. Assertion : The linear equations $x-2 y-3=0$ and $3 x+4 y-20=0$ have exactly one solution
Reason : The linear equations $2 x+3 y-9=0$ and $4 x+6 y-18=0$ have a unique solution

## Multiple Matching Question

DIRECTIONS : Following question has four statements ( $A, B, C$ and $D)$ given in Column-I and four statements ( $p, q, r, s, t$ and $u$ ) in Column-II. Any given statement in Column-I can have correct matching with one or more statement(s) given in Column-II.

## Integer Type Questions

DIRECTIONS : Answer the following questions. The answer to each of the questions is a single digit integer, ranging from 0 to 9.

1. For what value of $k$ will the following system of linear equations have no solutions?
$3 x+y=1$ and $(2 k-1) x+(k-1) y=2 k+1$
2. Find the sum of values of $a$ and $b$ for which the following system of linear equations has infinite number of solutions:

$$
\begin{gathered}
2 x+3 y=7 \\
(a+b+1) x+(a+2 b+2) y=4(a+b)+1
\end{gathered}
$$

3. The numerator of a fraction is 4 less than the denominator. If the numerator is decreased by 2 and the denominator is increased by 1 , then the denominator is eight times the numerator. The fraction is $\frac{a}{b}$. Find $\frac{a+b}{2}$.
4. For what value of $k$ will the system of linear equations have infinite number of solutions:
$k x+4 y=k-4,16 x+k y=k ?$

# SOLUTIONS <br> (Brief Explanations of Selected Questions) 

## Exercise 1

Master Boards

## Multiple Choice Questions

1. (a) If the lines are parallel, then
$\frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}}=\frac{b_{1}}{b_{2}} \neq \frac{c_{1}}{c_{2}}$
Here, $a_{1}=3, b_{1}=-1, c_{1}=-5, a_{2}=6, b_{2}=-2, c_{2}=-p$
$\Rightarrow \quad \frac{3}{6}=\frac{-1}{-2} \neq \frac{-5}{-p}$

Taking II and III part of equation (i), we get
$\Rightarrow \frac{1}{2} \neq \frac{-5}{-p} \Rightarrow-p \neq-10 \Rightarrow p \neq 10$
So, option (a) is correct.
2. (c) Here, $\frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}}=\frac{k}{12}, \frac{b_{1}}{b_{2}}=\frac{3}{k}, \frac{c_{1}}{c_{2}}=\frac{k-3}{k}$

For a pair of linear equations to have infinitely many solutions:

$$
\frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}}=\frac{b_{1}}{b_{2}}=\frac{c_{1}}{c_{2}}
$$

So, we have $\frac{k}{12}=\frac{3}{k}=\frac{k-3}{k}$ or $\frac{k}{12}=\frac{3}{k}$ which gives $k^{2}=36$ i.e., $k= \pm 6$
Also, $\frac{3}{k}=\frac{k-3}{k}$ gives $3 k=k^{2}-3 k$, i.e., $6 k=k^{2}$, which means $k=0$ or $k=6$.
Therefore, the value of $k$ that satisfies both the conditions, is $k=6$. For this value, the pair of linear equations has infinitely many solutions.
3. (c) For a pair of linear equations having unique solution
$\frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}} \neq \frac{b_{1}}{b_{2}} \Rightarrow \frac{3}{2 m-5} \neq \frac{-2}{7}$
or $-4 m+10 \neq 21$ or $-4 m \neq 11$ or $m \neq-\frac{11}{4}$
4. (b) Let length and breadth be $x \mathrm{~cm}$ and $y \mathrm{~cm}$ respectively.

According to problem,

$$
2(x+y)=40
$$

and $\frac{y}{x}=\frac{2}{3}$
on solving, $x=12, y=8$
$\therefore \quad$ length $=12 \mathrm{~cm}$ and breadth $=8 \mathrm{~cm}$.
5. (b) The pair of linear equations in two variables is also known as simultaneous equations.
6. (a) Let the two numbers be $x$ and $y(x>y)$. Then,

$$
\begin{aligned}
& x-y=26 \\
& x=3 y
\end{aligned}
$$

Substituting value of $x$ from equation (ii) in (i)

1. consistent
2. 4 and 1
3. Cannot be determined
4. 5
5. inconsistent.
6. positive
7. zero
8. Does not exist
$3 y-y=26 \Rightarrow 2 y=26 \Rightarrow y=13$
Substituting value of $y$ in equation (ii), $x=3 \times 13=39$
Thus, two numbers are 13 and 39 .
9. (b) Let the annual increment be $₹ y$ and initial salary be $₹ x$
$\therefore \quad x+4 y=4500$
and $x+10 y=5400$

Solving eqs. (i) and (ii), we get
$x=3900$ and $y=150 \therefore$ Initial salary $=₹ 3900$
and increment $=₹ 150$
8. (a) The pair of linear equations are
$x+2 y-5=0$ and $3 x+12 y-10=0$
Here, $a_{1}=1, b_{1}=2, c_{1}=-5$ and $a_{2}=3, b_{2}=12$,
$c_{2}=-10$. Now, $\frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}}=\frac{1}{3}, \frac{b_{1}}{b_{2}}=\frac{2}{12}=\frac{1}{6}$. As $\frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}} \neq \frac{b_{1}}{b_{2}}$
So, pair of equations has a unique solution.
9. (c) Let the no. of girls be ' $x$ ' and the no. of boys be ' $y$ '.

Given, $0.50 x+0.25 y=49$
and $x+y=150$
$\Rightarrow \frac{x}{2}+\frac{x}{4}=49$

From (i) \& (ii), $x=46, y=104$
Hence, number of boys $(y)=104$
10. (a) Let the age of father be ' $x$ ' years and the age of son be ' $y$ ' years
According to question, $x+y=65$
and $2(x-y)=50 \Rightarrow x-y=25$

Adding eqs. (i) and (ii), we get, $2 x=90 \Rightarrow x=45$
Hence, the age of father $=45$ years
11. (d) $\frac{1}{5}=\frac{2}{k} \neq \frac{-3}{7}$
$\Rightarrow \quad k=10$
$\left[\begin{array}{ll}\because & \text { For inconsistent } \\ & \frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}}=\frac{b_{1}}{b_{2}} \neq \frac{c_{1}}{c_{2}}\end{array}\right]$

## Very Short Answer Questions

1. To eliminate $y$, we have to make the coefficients of $y$ in given equations (i) and (ii) equal.
Multiplying (i) by 3, we get, $9 x+6 y=33$

Multiplying (ii) by 2 , we get, $4 x+6 y=8$

Subtracting (iv) from (iii), we have, $5 x=25 \quad \therefore \quad x=5$ From (i), $2 y=11-3 x=11-3(5)=-4$ i.e., $2 y=-4$ or $y=-2 \therefore x=5 ; y=-2$ is the solution.
2. For these two equations
$a_{1}=3, a_{2}=9, b_{1}=4, b_{2}=12, c_{1}=-8, c_{2}=-24$,
$\therefore \frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}}=\frac{b_{1}}{b_{2}}=\frac{c_{1}}{c_{2}}$. Since, $\frac{3}{9}=\frac{4}{12}=\frac{-8}{-24}$
The above pair of equations will have infinite solutions.
3. Given equations are
$x+5 y-7=0$ and $4 x+20 y-k=0$
Here, $\quad a_{1}=1, b_{1}=5, c_{1}=-7 ; a_{2}=4, b_{2}=20, c_{2}=k$ We know that equations represent coincident lines if they are consistent with many solutions.
$\frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}}=\frac{b_{1}}{b_{2}}=\frac{c_{1}}{c_{2}} \Rightarrow \frac{1}{4}=\frac{5}{20}=\frac{-7}{k} \Rightarrow \frac{1}{4}=\frac{-7}{k} \Rightarrow k=-28$
Hence, for $k=-28$, given equations represent coincident lines.
4. $a x+b=0 \Rightarrow x=-\frac{b}{a}$
5. $3 x+2 y-7=0$
$4 x+y-6=0$

From (ii), $y=6-4 x$
Put the value of $y$ in eqn. (i)

$$
\begin{array}{rlrl} 
& & 3 x+2 y-7 & =0 \\
\Rightarrow & 3 x+2(6-4 x)-7 & =0 \\
\Rightarrow & 3 x+12-8 x-7 & =0 \\
\Rightarrow & & 5 x & =5 \\
\therefore & & x & =1
\end{array}
$$

Substitute the value of $x$ in eqn. (ii), we get

$$
\begin{array}{rlrl} 
& & 4 x+y-6 & =0 \\
\Rightarrow & 4(1)+y-6 & =0 \\
\Rightarrow & 4+y-6 & =0 \\
\Rightarrow & y-2 & =0 \\
\therefore & y & =2
\end{array}
$$

Hence, values of $x$ and $y$ are 1 and 2 .
6. Here, $a_{1}=2, b_{1}=1, c_{1}=3$ and $a_{2}=4, b_{2}=2, c_{2}=6$

Clearly, $\frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}}=\frac{b_{1}}{b_{2}}=\frac{c_{1}}{c_{2}} ; \frac{2}{4}=\frac{1}{2}=\frac{3}{6} \Rightarrow \frac{1}{2}=\frac{1}{2}=\frac{1}{2}$
Hence, lines are coincident.
7.

Since, ABCD is a rectangle.

$$
\Rightarrow x+y=30
$$

[ ∵ opposite sides of a rectangle are equal]
Adding equations (i) and (ii), we get
$2 x=44$

Taking I and II
$\frac{3}{5}=\frac{-(a+1)}{1-2 a}$
$-5 a-5=3-6 a$
$-5 a+6 a=3+5$
$a=8$
$\therefore \quad a=8, b=5$

$$

10 b-5=9 b \\
10 b-9 b=5 \\
b=5

$x=\frac{44}{2}=22$
Put $x=22$ in equation (i)
