## CASE STUDY-2: Formation of Solid cone(s) when a right triangle rotates around one of its sides

(i) When a plane sheet in the form of a right angled triangle rotates about one of its side other than hypotenuse, then a solid cone is formed.
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-391.jpg?height=303&width=260&top_left_y=1589&top_left_x=441)

Let us consider a $\triangle \mathrm{ABC}$ right angle at B [in fig. (i)]. Now rotate this triangle along its side AB (perpendicular) which is other than hypotenuse, then a solid cone is formed as shown in the fig. (ii). In this case,
(a) Radius (r) of the cone $=$ Base (b) of the triangle.
(b) Height $(h)$ of the cone $=$ Perpendicular $(p)$ of the triangle and
(c) Slant height ( $l$ ) of the cone $=$ Hypotenuse ( $H$ ) of the triangle
(ii) When a plane sheet in the form of a right angled triangle rotates about its hypotenuse, two solid cones are formed.

![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-391.jpg?height=319&width=155&top_left_y=1897&top_left_x=1036)
h (Hypotenuse)
Fig. (iii)

![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-391.jpg?height=279&width=211&top_left_y=1579&top_left_x=1011)

Let us consider a triangle ABC right angled at A as shown in the fig. (iii). Now rotates this triangle about its hypotenuse BC, then two solid cones (joint together) are formed as shown in the fig. (iv). In this case,
(a) Radius ( $r$ ) of each cone $=$ Length of the perpendicular (AD) drawn to the hypotenuse BC from its opposite vertex A of the triangle ABC .
(b) Slant height ( $l_{1}$ ) of the first cone $=$ Base (b) of the $\triangle \mathrm{ABC}$.
(c) Slant height $\left(l_{2}\right)$ of the second cone $=$ Perpendicular $(p)$ of the $\triangle \mathrm{ABC}$
(d) Height $\left(h_{1}\right)$ of the first cone = BD
(e) Height $\left(h_{2}\right)$ of the second cone = CD

## Think Out of the Box

[Hint: Slant height is 13 cm and height is 12 cm . Ans. : $282.86 \mathrm{~cm}^{2}$ ]

CASE-I : A right triangle rotates about one side other than hypotenuse.

Illustration: A right triangle, whose sides are $3 \mathrm{~cm}, 4 \mathrm{~cm}$ and 5 cm is vevolve about its 4 cm side. Find the volume of the cone so formed. (use $\pi=3.14$ ).

Sol. Height $=4 \mathrm{~cm}$, radius $=3 \mathrm{~cm}$.

$$
\begin{aligned}
& \text { Volume of the cone }=\frac{1}{3} \pi r^{2} h \\
& =\frac{1}{3} \times 3.14 \times 3^{2} \times 4=37.68 \mathrm{~cm}^{3} .
\end{aligned}
$$

![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-391.jpg?height=243&width=209&top_left_y=406&top_left_x=564)

## CASE-II : A right triangle rotates about its hypotenuse.

Illustration: A right triangle, whose sides (other than hypotenuse) are 15 cm and 20 cm is revolve about its hypotenuse. Find the volume of the two cones so formed. (use $\pi=3.14$ )

Sol. In $\triangle A B C$ right angled at A ,

$$
B C=\sqrt{A B^{2}+A C^{2}}=\sqrt{20^{2}+15^{2}}=25 \mathrm{~cm}
$$

Slant height of first cone is 15 cm and of second cone is 20 cm . Radius of the two cones are same.

In the figure, let radius $=\mathrm{r} \mathrm{cm}$ and $O B=x \mathrm{~cm}$.
$x^{2}+r^{2}=15^{2} (25-x)^{2}+r^{2}=20^{2}$
[In rt. $\triangle A O B$ ]
[In rt. $\triangle A O C$ ]

From (i) and (ii), we get. $r=12 \mathrm{~cm}$ and $x=9 \mathrm{~cm}$.
∴ Volume of the two cones

$$
\begin{aligned}
& =\frac{1}{3} \pi r^{2} x+\frac{1}{3} \pi r^{2}(25-x) \\
& =\frac{1}{3} \times 3.14 \times(12)^{2} \times 25 \\
& =3768 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}
\end{aligned}
$$

![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-391.jpg?height=330&width=235&top_left_y=406&top_left_x=1344)
Q. 1 A kite which sides are $15 \mathrm{~cm}, 15 \mathrm{~cm}, 20 \mathrm{~cm}$ and 20 cm is rotates through longest diagonal. Find the volume of the two cones so formed.
[Hint: Slant height of first cone is 15 cm and second cone is 20 cm . Ans. : $3768 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$ ]
Q. 2 A right triangle which hypotenuse is 13 cm and other one side is 12 cm is rotated through the smallest side. Find the surface Area of the cone so formed.

## MISCELLANEOUS SOLVED EXAMPLES

1. The decorative block shown in figure is made of two solids-a cube and a hemisphere. The base of the block is a cube with edge 5 cm and the hemisphere fixed on the top has a diameter of 4.2 cm . Find the total surface area of the block. (Take $\boldsymbol{\pi} \boldsymbol{=} \mathbf{2 2} \boldsymbol{/} \boldsymbol{7}$ )
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-392.jpg?height=448&width=469&top_left_y=651&top_left_x=772)

Sol. Side of cube $\ell=5 \mathrm{~cm}$.
∴ Total surface area of cube $=6 \ell^{2}=6(5)^{2}=150 \mathrm{~cm}^{2}$. Radius of hemisphere, $r=\frac{4.2}{2}=2.1 \mathrm{~cm}$.
Surface area of the block $=$ TSA of cube + CSA of hemisphere - Base area of hemisphere
$=150+2 \pi r^{2}-\pi r^{2}=150+\pi r^{2}=150+\frac{22}{7} \times 2.1 \times 2.1=150+\frac{22}{7} \times 4.41=150+13.86=163.86 \mathrm{~cm}^{2}$
2. A solid is in the form of a cylinder with hemispherical ends. The total height of the solid is 19 cm and the diameter of the cylinder is $\mathbf{7 ~ c m}$. Find the volume and surface area of the solid.
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-392.jpg?height=451&width=280&top_left_y=1443&top_left_x=865)

Sol. Diameter of cylinder $=7 \mathrm{~cm}$.
∴ Radius of cylinder $(r)=3.5 \mathrm{~cm} . \quad \therefore \quad$ Radius of each hemispherical end $=3.5 \mathrm{~cm}$.
Height of solid $=19 \mathrm{~cm} . \quad \therefore \quad$ Height of cylinder $(h)=19-(3.5+3.5)=19-7=12 \mathrm{~cm}$.
Volume of cylinder $=\pi r^{2} h=\frac{22}{7} \times \frac{7}{2} \times \frac{7}{2} \times 12=462 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
Volume of each hemispherical end $=\frac{2}{3} \neq \mathrm{r}^{3}=\frac{2}{3} \times \frac{22}{7} \times \frac{7}{2} \times \frac{7}{2} \times \frac{7}{2}=\frac{539}{6} \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
Total volume of solid $=462+\frac{539}{6}+\frac{539}{6}=\frac{2772+539+539}{6}=\frac{3850}{6}=641.67 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
Curved surface area of cylinder $=2 \pi r h=2 \times \frac{22}{7} \times \frac{7}{2} \times 12=264 \mathrm{~cm}^{2}$
Curved surface area of each hemispherical end $=2 \pi r^{2}=2 \times \frac{22}{7} \times \frac{7}{2} \times \frac{7}{2}=77 \mathrm{~cm}^{2}$
$\therefore \quad$ Total surface area of solid $=264+77+77=418 \mathrm{~cm}^{2}$
3. A closed cylindrical container, the radius of which is 7 cm and height 10 cm is to be made out of a metal sheet. Find
(i) the area of metal sheet required.
(ii) the volume of the cylinder made.
(iii) the cost of painting the lateral surface of the cylinder at the rate of ₹ $\mathbf{4}$ per $\mathbf{c m}^{\mathbf{2}}$.

Sol. (i) The area of the metal sheet required $=$ The total surface area of the cylinder $=2 \pi \mathrm{r}(r+h)$

$$
=2 \times \frac{22}{7} \times 7(7+10)=44(17)=748 \mathrm{~cm}^{2} .
$$

(ii) Volume $=\pi r^{2} h=\frac{22}{7} \times 7 \times 7 \times 10=22 \times 70=1540 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
(iii) To find the cost of painting the lateral surface, we need to find the curved (lateral) surface area.

$$
\therefore \mathrm{LSA}=2 \pi r h=2 \times \frac{22}{7} \times 7 \times 10=440 \mathrm{~cm}^{2}
$$

Cost of painting $=440 \times 4=₹ 1760$.
4. Volume of two cubes are in the ratio $1: 27$, find the ratio of their surface areas.

Sol. Let sides of two cubes be $a_{1}$ and $a_{2}$ respectively.
Volume of first cube $\left(V_{1}\right)=a_{1}^{3}$ and Volume of second cube $\left(V_{2}\right)=a_{2}^{3}$

$$
\therefore \quad \frac{V_{1}}{V_{2}}=\frac{a_{1}^{3}}{a_{2}^{3}}=\left(\frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}}\right)^{3}=\frac{1}{27} \quad \therefore \quad \frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}}=\frac{1}{3}
$$

Now, surface area of first cube $\left(S_{1}\right)=6 a_{1}^{2}$ and surface area of second cube $\left(S_{2}\right)=6 a_{2}^{2}$

$$
\begin{aligned}
\text { Now, } \frac{S_{1}}{S_{2}} & =\frac{6 a_{1}^{2}}{6 a_{2}^{2}}=\left(\frac{a_{1}}{a_{2}}\right)^{2} \\
\frac{S_{1}}{S_{2}} & =\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)^{2} \quad(\text { from equation (i)) } \\
\frac{S_{1}}{S_{2}} & =\frac{1}{9} \quad \therefore \quad \text { Ratio of surface are }=1: 9 .
\end{aligned}
$$

5. The height of a cylinder is 80 cm and diameter of the base is 14 cm . Find its curved surface area, total surface area and volume.
Sol. Height $(h)=80 \mathrm{~cm}$, Diameter of base $=14 \mathrm{~cm} \quad \therefore \quad$ radius of base $(r)=\frac{14}{2}=7 \mathrm{~cm}$
$\therefore \quad$ curved surface area of cylinder $=2 \pi r h=2 \times \frac{22}{7} \times 7 \times 80=44 \times 80=3520 \mathrm{sq} . \mathrm{cm}$
Total surface area (TSA) = C.S.A + Area of two ends
$=2 \pi r h+2 \pi r^{2}=2 \pi r(h+r)=2 \times \frac{22}{7} \times 7 \times(80+7)=2 \times 22 \times 87$
T.S.A of cylinder $=3828 \mathrm{sq} . \mathrm{cm}$
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-393.jpg?height=403&width=344&top_left_y=1612&top_left_x=1533)

Volume (V) of cylinder $=\pi r^{2} h=\frac{22}{7} \times(7)^{2} \times 80$

$$
=22 \times 7 \times 80=154 \times 80=12320 \text { cubic units. }
$$

6. Find the slant height, curved surface area and the whole surface area of a cone of radius 21 cm and height 28 cm .

Sol. Here, $r=21 \mathrm{~cm}$ and $h=28 \mathrm{~cm}$.
∴ Slant height, $l=\sqrt{r^{2}+h^{2}}=\sqrt{(21)^{2}+(28)^{2}}=\sqrt{1225}=35 \mathrm{~cm}$
Curved surface area $=\pi r l=\left(\frac{22}{7} \times 21 \times 35\right) \mathrm{cm}^{2}=2310 \mathrm{~cm}^{2}$
Total surface area $=\left(\pi r l+\pi r^{2}\right)=\left(2310+\frac{22}{7} \times 21 \times 21\right) \mathrm{cm}^{2}=3696 \mathrm{~cm}^{2}$
7. A hemispherical tank of radius $1 \frac{3}{4} \mathrm{~m}$ is full of water. It is connected with a pipe which empties it at the rate of $\mathbf{7}$ liters per second. How much time will it take to empty the tank completely?
Sol. Radius of the hemisphere $=\frac{7}{4} \mathrm{~m}=\frac{7}{4} \times 100 \mathrm{~cm}=175 \mathrm{~cm}$
∴ Volume of the hemisphere $=\frac{2}{3} \times \pi \times 175 \times 175 \times 175 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
The cylindrical pipe empties it at the rate of 7 litres i.e., $7000 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$ of water per second.
Hence, the required time to empty the tank $=\left(\frac{2}{3} \times \frac{22}{7} \times 175 \times 175 \times 175 \div 7000\right) \mathrm{sec}$

$$
=\frac{2}{3} \times \frac{22}{7} \times \frac{175 \times 175 \times 175}{7000 \times 60} \mathrm{~min}=\frac{11 \times 25 \times 7}{3 \times 2 \times 12} \mathrm{~min}=\frac{1925}{72} \mathrm{~min} \approx 26.74 \mathrm{~min} \text { nearly. }
$$

8. Water in a canal, 30 dm wide and 12 dm deep, is flowing with a speed of $10 \mathrm{~km} /$ hour. How much area will it irrigate in $\mathbf{3 0 ~ m i n u t e s , ~ i f ~} \mathbf{8 ~ c m}$. of standing water is required for irrigation.
Sol. Speed of water in the canal $=10 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{h}=10000 \mathrm{~m} / 60 \mathrm{~min}=\frac{500}{3} \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{min}$.
∴ The volume of the water flowing out of the canal in 1 minute $=\left(\frac{500}{3} \times \frac{30}{10} \times \frac{12}{10}\right) \mathrm{m}^{3}=600 \mathrm{~m}^{3}$
$\therefore \quad$ In 30 min , the amount of water flowing out of the canal $=(600 \times 30) \mathrm{m}^{3}=18000 \mathrm{~m}^{3}$
If the required area of the irrigated land is $x m^{2}$, then the volume of water to be needed to irrigate the land
$=\left(x \times \frac{8}{100}\right) \mathrm{m}^{3}=\frac{2 x}{25} \mathrm{~m}^{3}$
Hence, $\frac{2 x}{25}=18000 \Rightarrow x=18000 \times \frac{25}{2}=225000$
Hence, the required area is $225000 \mathrm{~m}^{2}$
9. The earth dug out of a well is spread evenly all around it to form an embankment which is 2 m wide. Find the height of the embankment if the diameter of the well is 2 m and its depth is 20 m .
Sol. Given, diameter of well $=2 \mathrm{~m}$ and depth of well $=20 \mathrm{~m} \quad \therefore \quad$ Volume of earth dug out $=\pi\left(\frac{2}{2}\right)^{2} \times 20=20 \pi$
Given, that the width of the embankment is 2 m . Let the height of the embankment be $h$.
Then, volume of embankment $=$ external volume - internal volume $=\pi(3)^{2} h-\pi(1)^{2} h=8 \pi h$
But, volume of embankment $=$ volume of earth taken out $\quad \therefore 8 \pi h=20 \pi \quad \therefore \quad h=\frac{20}{8}=\frac{5}{2}=2.5 \mathrm{~m}$
10. A piece of copper of volume $1 \mathrm{~m}^{3}$ is melted down and recast into a bar which is 36 m long and has a square crosssection. An exact cube is cut off from one end of the bar. Find the cost of this cube, if the original piece of copper cost ₹ $\mathbf{1 0 , 8 0 0}$.
Sol. Given, volume of $\mathrm{bar}=1 \mathrm{~m}^{2}$ and length of $\mathrm{bar}=36 \mathrm{~m}$
$\therefore \quad$ Area of cross-section $=\frac{\text { volume }}{\text { length }}=\frac{1}{36} \mathrm{~m}^{2} \quad \therefore \quad$ Side of square cross-section $=\frac{1}{6} \mathrm{~m}$
∴ $\quad$ Volume of cube cut off from bar $=\left(\frac{1}{6}\right)^{3}=\frac{1}{216} \mathrm{~m}^{3}$
Cost of $1 \mathrm{~m}^{3}$ of copper $=₹ 10,800$ (Given) $\quad \therefore \quad$ cost of cube $=\frac{1}{216} \times 10800=₹ 50$
11. Hanumappa and his wife Gangamma are busy making jaggery out of sugarcane juice. They have processed the sugarcane juice to make the molasses, which is poured into moulds in the shape of a frustum of a cone having the diameters of its two circular faces as $\mathbf{3 0 ~ c m}$ and $\mathbf{3 5}$ cm and the vertical height of the mould is 14 cm (see Fig.). If each cm3 of molasses has mass about 1.2 g , find the mass of the molasses that can be poured into each mould. (Take $\boldsymbol{\pi}=22 / 7$ )
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-395.jpg?height=215&width=314&top_left_y=395&top_left_x=1546)

Sol. Since, the mould is in the shape of a frustum of a cone, the quantity (volume) of molasses that can be poured into it is $\frac{1}{3} \pi h\left(r_{1}^{2}+r_{2}^{2}+r_{1} r_{2}\right)$, where $r_{1}$ is the radius of the larger base and $r_{2}$ is the radius of the smaller base.
$\Rightarrow \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{22}{7} \times 14\left[\left(\frac{35}{2}\right)^{2}+\left(\frac{30}{2}\right)^{2}+\left(\frac{35}{2} \times \frac{30}{2}\right)\right] \mathrm{cm}^{3}=11641.7 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
It is given that $1 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$ of molasses has mass 1.2 g .
So, the mass of the molasses that can be poured into each mould

$$
=(11641.7 \times 1.2) \mathrm{g}=13970.04 \mathrm{~g}=13.97 \mathrm{~kg}=14 \mathrm{~kg} \text { (approx.) }
$$

12. A bucket is 40 cm in diameter at the top and 28 cm in diameter at the bottom. Find the capacity of the bucket in litres, if it is $\mathbf{2 1 ~ c m}$ deep. Also, find the cost of tin sheet used in making the bucket, if the cost of tin is ₹ $\mathbf{1 . 5 0}$ per sq. dm.
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-395.jpg?height=476&width=549&top_left_y=1062&top_left_x=790)

Sol. Given : $r_{1}=20 \mathrm{~cm}, r_{2}=14 \mathrm{~cm}$ and $h=21 \mathrm{~cm}$. Now, the required capacity (i.e., volume) of the bucket $=\frac{\pi h^{3}}{3}\left(r_{1}^{2}+r_{1} r_{2}+r_{2}^{2}\right)$

$$
=\frac{22 \times(21)^{3}}{7 \times 3}\left(20^{2}+20 \times 14+14^{2}\right) \mathrm{cm}^{3}=22 \times 876 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}=19272 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}=\frac{19272}{1000} \text { litres }=19.272 \text { litres }
$$

Now, $\ell=\sqrt{\left(r_{1}-r_{2}\right)^{2}+h^{2}}=\sqrt{(20-14)^{2}+21^{2} \mathrm{~cm}}=\sqrt{6^{2}+21^{2}} \mathrm{~cm}=\sqrt{36+441} \mathrm{~cm}=\sqrt{477} \mathrm{~cm} \approx 21.84 \mathrm{~cm}$.
∴ Total surface area of the bucket (which is open at the top) $=\pi \ell\left(r_{1}+r_{2}\right)+\pi r_{2}^{2}$

$$
=\pi\left[\left(r_{1}+r_{2}\right) \ell+r_{2}^{2}\right]=\frac{22}{7}\left[(20+14) \times 21.84+14^{2}\right]=2949.76 \mathrm{~cm}^{2}
$$

∴ Required cost of the tin sheet at the rate of ₹ 1.50 per $\mathrm{dm}^{2}$ i.e., per $100 \mathrm{~cm}^{2}=₹ \frac{1.50 \times 2949.76}{100}=₹ 44.25$
13. Water flows at the rate of $\mathbf{5 m}$ per minute through a cylindrical pipe having its diameter as $\mathbf{5 m m}$. How much time will it take to fill a conical vessel whose diameter of the base is 40 cm and depth 24 cm .
Sol. Diameter of the pipe $=5 \mathrm{~mm}=\frac{5}{10} \mathrm{~cm}=\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{~cm} . \quad \therefore$. Radius of the pipe $=\frac{1}{2} \times \frac{1}{2} \mathrm{~cm}=\frac{1}{4} \mathrm{~cm}$
In 1 minute, the length of the water column in the cylindrical pipe $=10 \mathrm{~m}=1000 \mathrm{~cm}$.
$\therefore \quad$ Volume of water that flows out of the pipe in 1 minute $=\pi \times \frac{1}{4} \times \frac{1}{4} \times 1000 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
Also, volume of the cone $=\frac{1}{3} \times \pi \times 20 \times 24 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$. Hence, the time needed to fill up this conical vessel
$=\left(\frac{20 \times 20 \times 24}{3} \times \frac{4 \times 4}{1000}\right)=\frac{4 \times 24 \times 16}{30}$ minutes $=\frac{256}{5}$ minutes $=51.2$ minutes
Hence, the required time is 51.2 minutes.
CONNECTING TOPIC CONNECTING TOPIC CONNECTING TOPIC CONNECTING TOPIC CONNECTING TOPIC CONNECTING TOPIC

## SOLVED EXAMPLE BASED ON CONNECTING TOPICS

14. The diagram shows a right pyramid that has an isosceles triangular base. If the volume of the pyramid is $330 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$, calculate its height, $h$.
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-396.jpg?height=528&width=385&top_left_y=539&top_left_x=842)

Sol. $A D=\sqrt{13^{2}-5^{2}}=\sqrt{144}=12 \mathrm{~cm}$
Area of base $=\frac{1}{2} \times B C \times A D=\frac{1}{2} \times 10 \times 12=60 \mathrm{~cm}^{2}$
Volume of pyramid $=\frac{1}{3}$ (Area of base) × Height
$330=\frac{1}{3} \times 60 \times h \Rightarrow 20 h=330$
$\therefore h=\frac{330}{20}=16.5 \mathrm{~cm}$
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-396.jpg?height=423&width=314&top_left_y=1087&top_left_x=1503)
5. Calculate the surface area of the following prism.
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-396.jpg?height=346&width=483&top_left_y=1576&top_left_x=829)

Sol. The given prism has 2 triangles and 3 rectangles on its surfaces.
Area of two triangles $=2\left[\frac{1}{2} \times 8 \times 5\right]$
Area of rectangle type $P=2(10 \times 6)$
Area of rectangle type $Q=8 \times 10$
∴ Total surface area $=$ area of two triangles + area of three rectangles
$=2\left(\frac{1}{2} \times 8 \times 5\right)+2(10 \times 6)+(8 \times 10)=40+120+80=240 \mathrm{~cm}^{2}$.
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-396.jpg?height=487&width=552&top_left_y=1955&top_left_x=1265)

## Multiple Choice Questions

DIRECTIONS : This section contains multiple choice questions. Each question has 4 choices (a), (b), (c) and (d) out of which only one is correct.

1. The volume of a cylinder is $448 \pi \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$ and height 7 cm . Find its lateral surface area and total surface area is
(a) $253 \mathrm{~cm}^{2}$
(b) $352 \mathrm{~cm}^{2}$
(c) $532 \mathrm{~cm}^{2}$
(d) $325 \mathrm{~cm}^{2}$
2. The diameter of a garden roller is 1.4 m , and 2 m long. How much area will it cover in 5 revolutions.
(a) $44 \mathrm{~m}^{2}$
(b) $140 \mathrm{~m}^{2}$
(c) $440 \mathrm{~m}^{2}$
(d) $220 \mathrm{~m}^{2}$
3. If a sphere and a cube have equal surface areas, then the ratio of the diameter of the sphere to the edge of the cube is
(a) $1: 2$
(b) $2: 1$
(c) $\sqrt{\neq}: \sqrt{6}$
(d) $\sqrt{6}: \sqrt{\neq}$
4. If the radii of the circular ends of a conical bucket, which is 45 cm high are 28 cm and 7 cm . Find the capacity of the bucket.
(a) $48500 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
(b) $48510 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
(c) $48000 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
(d) $48200 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
5. The internal and external diameter of a hollow hemispherical vessel are 42 cm and 45.5 cm . respectively. Find its capacity (volume) and also its outer curved surface area.
(a) 5.27 litres, $3253.25 \mathrm{~cm}^{2}$
(b) 5.20 litres, $3253.25 \mathrm{~cm}^{2}$
(c) 5.27 litres, $3200.18 \mathrm{~cm}^{2}$
(d) 5.27 litres, $3250.25 \mathrm{~cm}^{2}$
6. If $h, c, v$ are respectively the height, the C.S.A and the volume of a cone, find the value of $3 \pi v h^{3}-c^{2} h^{2}+9 v^{2}$
(a) 1
(b) 2
(c) 0
(d) 3
7. The diagram shows the parts of a right cylinder. The volume of the cylinder, in $\mathrm{cm}^{3}$ is
(a) $\frac{20}{\pi}$
(b) $\frac{50}{\pi}$
(c) $\frac{25}{\pi}$
(d) $40 \pi$
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-397.jpg?height=332&width=328&top_left_y=1905&top_left_x=726)
8. If $h$ be the height and $\alpha$ the semi-vertical angle of a right circular cone, then its volume is given by
(a) $\frac{1}{3} \pi h^{3} \tan ^{2} \alpha$
(b) $\frac{1}{3} \pi h^{2} \tan ^{2} \alpha$
(c) $\frac{1}{3} \pi h^{2} \tan ^{3} \alpha$
(d) $\frac{1}{3} \pi h^{3} \tan ^{3} \alpha$
9. Cubes $A, B, C$ having edges of $18 \mathrm{~cm}, 24 \mathrm{~cm}$ and 30 cm respectively are melted and moulded into a new cube $D$. Find the edge of the bigger cube $D$.
(a) 32
(b) 28
(c) 39
(d) 36
10. The height of a conical tent is 14 m and its floor area is $346.5 \mathrm{~m}^{2}$. The length of canvas, 11 m wide, required for it is.
(a) 490 m
(b) 525 m
(c) 665 m
(d) 860 m

## True/False

DIRECTIONS : Read the following statements and write your answer as true or false.

1. Volume of the solid is measured in cubic units.
2. Area is the length of the boundary of a closed figure.
3. Area is the total surface covered by a closed figure.
4. The volume of sphere of diameter is $\frac{\pi d^{3}}{6}$.
5. The total surface area of a solid cylinder of radius $r$ and height $h$ is $2 \pi r(h+r)$.
6. If a right circular cone and a cylinder have equal circles as their base and have equal heights, then the ratio of their volumes is $2: 3$
7. If the base area and the volume of a cone are numerically equal, then its height is 3 units.
8. If the curved surface of a right circular cylinder is $1760 \mathrm{~cm}^{2}$ and its radius is 21 cm , then its height is $\frac{80}{3} \mathrm{~cm}$.
9. If the total surface area of a cube is $\frac{50}{3} \mathrm{~m}^{2}$, then its side is $(5 / 3) \mathrm{m}$.
10. Surface area of a square pyramid is $S=s^{2}+2 s l$.

## Very Short Answer Questions

1. An underground water tank is in the form of a cuboid of edges $48 \mathrm{~m}, 36 \mathrm{~m}$ and 28 m . Find the volume of the tank.
2. How many 3 metres cubes can be cut from a cuboid measuring $18 \mathrm{~m} \times 12 \mathrm{~m} \times 9 \mathrm{~m}$ ?
3. If a rectangular paper of length 6 cm and width 3 cm is rolled to form a cylinder with height equal to the width of the paper, then find its base radius.
4. Find the percentage increase in the volume of a cuboid when the three edges are increased by $100 \%, 150 \%$ and $250 \%$ respectively.
5. Two cylindrical cans have bases of the same size. The diameter of each is 14 cm . One of the cans is 10 cm
high and the other is 20 cm high. Find the ratio of their volumes.
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-398.jpg?height=378&width=449&top_left_y=443&top_left_x=1192)
6. How many metres of cloth 5 m wide will be required to make a conical tent, the radius of whose base is 7 m and whose height is 24 m ?

$$
\left(\text { Take } \pi=\frac{22}{7}\right)
$$

7. A joker's cap is in the form of a cone of radius 7 cm and height 24 cm . Find the area of the cardboard required to make the cap.
8. If the ratio of the radii of two cylinders is $2: 1$ and the ratio of their heights is $1: 2$, then compare their volumes.
9. There are two cones. The curved surface area of one is twice that of the other. The slant height of the latter is twice that of the former. Find the ratio of their radii.
10. A cone of height 15 cm and diameter 7 cm is mounted on a hemisphere of the same radius. Find the volume of the solid formed by the cone and the hemisphere.
11. A rectangular sheet of paper $44 \mathrm{~cm} \times 18 \mathrm{~cm}$ is rolled along its length and a cylinder is formed, find volume of the cylinder.
12. A hollow sphere of external and internal diameter are 8 cm and 4 cm , respectively is melted into a cone of base diameter 8 cm . Find height of cone.
13. How many lead balls, each of radius 1 cm can be made from a sphere whose radius is 8 cm ?
14. The volume of a right circular cylinder with its height equal to the radius is $25 \frac{1}{7} \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$. Find the height of the cylinder. (Use $\pi=\frac{22}{7}$ )

## Short Answer Questions

1. A circus tent is made of canvas and is in the form of a right circular cylinder and a right circular cone above it. The diameter and height of the cylindrical part of the tent are 126 m and 5 m respectively. The total height of the tent is 21 m . Find the total cost of tent if the canvas used costs $₹ 12$ per square metre.
2. Cubes $A, B, C$ having edges of $18 \mathrm{~cm}, 24 \mathrm{~cm}$ and 30 cm respectively, are melted and moulded into a new cube $D$. Find the edge of the bigger cube $D$.
3. A right circular cone and a right circular cylinder have equal heights and equal bases. Their respective curved surfaces are in the ratio $5: 8$. Find the ratio of their base radius to the height.
4. Keeping the volume of a wire the same as before, if we decrease its diameter by $5 \%$, then find the percent change in its length.
5. If the radius of the sphere is increased by $100 \%$, then what is the percentage increase in volume of the corresponding sphere.
6. The diameter of a copper sphere is 6 cm . The sphere is melted and recast into a wire. If the length of the wire be 36 cm , find its radius.
7. The diameters of two cones are equal. If their slant height are in the ratio $5: 4$, find the ratio of their curved surface areas.
8. Determine the ratio of the volume of a cube to that of a sphere which will exactly fit inside the cube.
9. Observe the following figure and find the total volume of the haystack.
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-399.jpg?height=362&width=378&top_left_y=995&top_left_x=461)
10. (a) Each edge of cube is increased by $50 \%$. Find the percentage increase in the surface area of the cube.
(b) A solid cube is cut into two cuboids of equal volumes. Find the ratio of the total surface area of the given cube and that of one of the cuboids.
11. A military tent is in the form of a right circular cone of vertical height 6 m . The diameter of the base being 7 m . If 12 soldiers can sleep in it, find the average cubic metres of air space required per soldier.
12. A hemispherical bowl of internal diameter 36 cm contains liquid. This liquid is filled into 72 cylindrical bottles of diameter 6 cm . Find the height of the each bottle, if $10 \%$ liquid is wasted in this transfer.
13. A sphere of diameter 12 cm , is dropped in a right circular cylindrical vessel, partly filled with water. If the sphere is completely submerged in water, the water level in the cylindrical vessel rises by $3 \frac{5}{9} \mathrm{~cm}$. Find the diameter of
the cylindrical vessel. the cylindrical vessel.
14. The slant height of a frustum of a cone is 4 cm and the perimeters of its circular ends are 18 cm and 6 cm . Find the curved surface area of the frustum.
15. Water in a canal, 6 m wide and 1.5 m deep, is flowing with a speed of $10 \mathrm{~km} /$ hour . How much area will it irrigate in 30 minutes; if 8 cm standing water is needed?

## Long Answer Questions

1. A solid right-circular cylinder has a base radius of 12 cm and height of 16 cm . It is melted and made into eight spherical balls of equal size. Find the radius of each ball.
2. The internal and external diameters of a hollow hemispherical vessel are 21 cm and 25.2 cm respectively. The cost of painting 1 sq. cm of the surface is 10 paise. Find the total cost of painting the entire vessel.
3. Find the volume of the largest right circular cone that can be cut out of a cube whose edge is 21 cm .
4. A toy is in the form of cone mounted on a hemisphere of common base radius 7 cm . The total height of the toy is 31 cm . Find the total surface area of the toy.
[Use $\pi=22 / 7$ ]
5. A right circular cone of height h is cut by a plane parallel to the base at a distance $\mathrm{h} / 3$ from the base, then find the ratio of the volumes of the resulting cone and the frustrum.
6. The outer and inner diameters of a hemispherical bowl are 17 cm and 15 cm respectively. Find the cost of polishing it all over at 25 paise per $\mathrm{cm}^{2}$. (Take $\pi=22 / 7$ ).
7. A solid toy is in the form of a hemisphere surmounted by a right circular cone. The height of the cone is 2 cm and the diameter of the base is 4 cm . Determine the volume of the toy. If a right circular cylinder circumscribes the toy (shown in figure), find the difference of the volumes of the cylinder and the toy. (Take $\pi=3.14$ )
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-399.jpg?height=296&width=209&top_left_y=1242&top_left_x=1370)
8. A vessel in the form of an inverted cone is filled with water to the brim. Its height is 20 cm and diameter is 16.8 cm . Two equal solid cones are dropped in it so that they are fully submerged. As a result, one third of the water in the original cone overflows. What is the volume of each of the solid cones submerged?
9. Length of a class-room is two times its height and its breadth is $1 \frac{1}{2}$ times its height. The cost of white-washing the walls at the rate of ₹ 1.60 per $\mathrm{m}^{2}$ is ₹ 179.20 . Find the cost of tiling the floor at the rate of ₹ 6.75 per $\mathrm{m}^{2}$.
10. Calculate the total surface area of each of the following combined solids. $\left(\pi=\frac{22}{7}\right)$

(a)
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-399.jpg?height=359&width=316&top_left_y=2161&top_left_x=1197)

(b)
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-399.jpg?height=364&width=150&top_left_y=2161&top_left_x=1571)

11. An open metal bucket is in the shape of a frustum of a cone, mounted on a hollow cylindrical base made of the same metallic sheet. The diameters of the two circular ends of the bucket are 45 cm and 25 cm , the total vertical height of the bucket is 40 cm and that of the cylindrical base is 6 cm . Find the area of the metallic sheet used to make the bucket, where we do not take into account the handle of the bucket. Also, find the volume of water the bucket can hold.
12. From each end of a solid metal cylinder, metal was scooped out in hemispherical form of same diameter. The height of the cylinder is 10 cm and its base is of radius 4.2 cm . The rest of the cylinder is melted and converted into a cylindrical wire of 1.4 cm thickness. Find the length of the wire. [Use $\pi=\frac{22}{7}$ ]
13. A bucket open at the top is in the form of a frustum of a cone with a capacity of $12308.8 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$. The radii of the top and bottom circular ends are 20 cm and 12 cm respectively. Find the height of the bucket and the area of metal sheet used in making the bucket. (use $\pi=3.14$ )
14. In a hospital used water is collected in a cylindrical tank of diameter 2 m and height 5 m . After recycling, this water is used to irrigate a park of hospital whose length is 25 m and breadth is 20 m . If tank is filled completely then what will be the height of standing water used for irrigating the park. Write your views on recycling of water.
15. The diameters of the lower and upper ends of a bucket in the form of a frustum of a cone are 10 cm and 30 cm respectively. If its height is 24 cm , find :
(i) The area of the metal sheet used to make the bucket.
(ii) Why we should avoid the bucket made by ordinary plastic? [Use $\pi=3.14]$
16. Find the curved surface area of the frustum of a cone, the diameters of whose circular ends are 20 m and 6 m and its height is 24 m .

## Reasoning Based Questions

1. In a right prism, the base is an equilateral triangle. Its volume is $80 \sqrt{3} \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$ and its lateral surface area is $240 \mathrm{~cm}^{2}$. Find its height (in cm).
2. Curved surface area of a conical cup is $154 \sqrt{2} \mathrm{~cm}^{2}$ and base radius is 7 cm . Find the angle at the vertex of the conical cup.
3. ABCD is a square of side 4 cm . If E is a point in the interior of the square such that $\triangle \mathrm{CED}$ is equilateral, then find the area of $\triangle \mathrm{ACE}\left(\mathrm{in} \mathrm{cm}^{2}\right)$.
4. Three solid cubes have a face diagonal of $4 \sqrt{2} \mathrm{~cm}$ each. Three other solid cubes have a face diagonal of $8 \sqrt{2} \mathrm{~m}$ each. All the cubes are melted together to form a cube. Find the side of cube formed (in cm).
5. The outer radius and inner radius of a 30 cm long cylindrical gold pipe are 14 cm and 7 cm respectively. It is filled with bronze. The densities of gold and bronze are $20 \mathrm{gm} / \mathrm{cm}^{3}$ and $30 \mathrm{gm} / \mathrm{cm}^{3}$ respectively. Find the weight of the cyclinder formed (in gm).

## HOTS Questions

1. Find the volume and total surface area of a tumbler in the form of a frustum of a cone, if the diameter of the ends are 6.50 cm and 3.50 cm and the perpendicular height of the tumbler is 7.80 cm .
2. A sector of a circle of radius 6 cm has an angle of $120^{\circ}$. It is rolled up so that the two bounding radii are joined together to form a cone. Find volume of cone and T.S.A of the cone.
3. A sphere of diameter 5 cm is dropped into a cylindrical vessel partly filled with water. The diameter of the base of the vessel is 10 cm . If the sphere is completely submerged, by how much will the level of water rise?
4. A copper rod of diameter 1 cm and length 8 cm is drawn into a wire of length 18 m of uniform thickness. Find the thickness of wire.

## Case Study Based MCQs

1. Mathematics teacher of a school took her $10^{\text {th }}$ standard students to show Red fort. It was a part of their Educational trip. The teacher had interest in history as well. She narrated the facts of Red fort to students. Then the teacher said in this monument one can find combination of solid figures. There are 2 pillars which are cylindrical in shape. Also 2 domes at the corners which are hemispherical. 7 smaller domes at the centre. Flag hoisting ceremony on Independence Day takes place near these domes.
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-400.jpg?height=333&width=771&top_left_y=1685&top_left_x=1032)
(i) How much cloth material will be required to cover 2 big domes each of radius 2.5 metres?
(Take $\pi=22 / 7$ )
(a) $75 \mathrm{~m}^{2}$
(b) $78.57 \mathrm{~m}^{2}$
(c) $87.47 \mathrm{~m}^{2}$
(d) $25.8 \mathrm{~m}^{2}$
(ii) Write the formula to find the volume of a cylindrical pillar.
(a) $\pi r^{2} h$
(b) $\pi \mathrm{rl}$
(c) $\pi r(1+r)$
(d) $2 \pi \mathrm{r}$
(iii) Find the lateral surface area of two pillars if height of the pillar is 7 m and radius of the base is 1.4 m .
(a) $112.3 \mathrm{~cm}^{2}$
(b) $123.2 \mathrm{~m}^{2}$
(c) $90 \mathrm{~m}^{2}$
(d) $345.2 \mathrm{~cm}^{2}$
(iv) How much is the volume of a hemisphere if the radius of the base is 3.5 m ?
(a) $85.9 \mathrm{~m}^{3}$
(b) $80 \mathrm{~m}^{3}$
(c) $98 \mathrm{~m}^{3}$
(d) $89.83 \mathrm{~m}^{3}$
(v) What is the ratio of sum of volumes of two hemispheres of radius 1 cm each to the volume of a sphere of radius 2 cm ?
(a) $1: 1$
(b) $1: 8$
(c) $8: 1$
(d) $1: 16$
2. A group of 5 students of class 10 went to an ice-cream parlor for the taste of ice-cream. Further, they decided to analyse the volume of ice-cream. In the figure, the ice-cream container is in the form of cone surmounted with ice-cream in hemispherical shape.
The radii of cone and hemispherical shape are equal to 3 cm and slant height of container is 5 cm .
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-401.jpg?height=369&width=204&top_left_y=785&top_left_x=847)

Answer the following questions.
(i) What is the formula to find the volume of hemisphere?
(a) $\frac{4}{3} \pi r^{2} h$
(b) $\frac{3}{4} \pi r^{2} h$
(c) $\frac{2}{3} \pi r^{3}$
(d) $\frac{3}{2} \pi r^{2} h$
(ii) What is the volume of ice-cream in hemispherical shape?
(a) $18 \pi \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
(b) $24 \pi \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
(c) $27 \pi \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
(d) $20 \pi \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
(iii) What is the formula to find the volume of cone?
(a) $\frac{2}{3} \pi r^{2} h$
(b) $\frac{1}{3} \pi r^{2} h$
(c) $\frac{4}{3} \pi r^{2} h$
(d) $\frac{2}{3} \pi \mathrm{rh}^{2}$
(iv) What is the volume of ice-cream in the container?
(a) $12 \pi \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
(b) $16 \pi \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
(c) $20 \pi \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
(d) $24 \pi \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
(v) If the cost of filling ice-cream in the container is ₹7 per centimeter cube, then find the total cost of icecream in the container and surmounted part.
(a) ₹ 640
(b) ₹ 660
(c) ₹ 670
(d) ₹ 760

## Exercise 2 TV Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar)

## Text-book Questions

1. A vessel is in the form of a hollow hemisphere mounted by a hollow cylinder. The diameter of the hemisphere is 14 cm and the total height of the vessel is 13 cm . Find the inner surface area of the vessel.
2. A cubical block of side 7 cm is surmounted by a hemisphere. What is the greatest diameter the hemisphere can have? Find the surface area of the solid.
3. A hemispherical depression is cut out from one face of a cubical wooden block such that the diameter $\ell$ of the hemisphere is equal to the edge of the cube. Determine the surface area of the remaining solid.
4. A medicine capsule is in the shape of a cylinder with two hemisphere stuck to each of its ends (see fig.). The length of the entire capsule is 14 mm and the diameter of the capsule is 5 mm . Find its surface area.
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-401.jpg?height=131&width=310&top_left_y=2110&top_left_x=497)
5. From a solid cylinder whose height is 2.4 cm and diameter 1.4 cm , a conical cavity of the same height and same diameter is hollowed out. Find the total surface area of the remaining solid to the nearest $\mathrm{cm}^{2}$.
6. Rachel, an engineering student, was asked to make a model shaped like a cylinder with two cones attached at its two ends by using a thin aluminium sheet. The diameter of the model is 3 cm and its length is 12 cm . If each cone
has a height of 2 cm , find the volume of air contained in the model that Rachel made. (Assume the outer and inner dimensions of the model to be nearly the same).
7. A solid consisting of a right circular cone of height 120 cm and radius 60 cm standing on a hemisphere of radius 60 cm is placed upright in a right circular cylinder full of water such that it touches the bottom. Find the volume of water left in the cylinder, if the radius of the cylinder is 60 cm and its height is 180 cm .
8. A well of diameter 3 m is dug 14 m deep. The earth taken out of it has been spread evenly all around it in the shape of a circular ring of width 4 m to form an embankment. Find the height of the embankment.
9. Water in a canal, 6 m wide and 1.5 m deep, is flowing with a speed of $10 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{h}$. How much area will it irrigate in 30 minutes, if 8 cm of standing water is needed?
10. A farmer connects a pipe of internal diameter 20 cm from a canal into a cylindrical tank in her field, which is 10 m in diameter and 2 m deep. If water flows through the pipe at the rate of $3 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{h}$, in how much time will the tank be filled?
11. Afez, the cap used by the Turks, is shaped like the frustum of a cone (see Fig.). If its radius on the open side is 10 cm , radius at the upper base is 4 cm and its slant height is 15 cm , find the area of material used for making it.
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-401.jpg?height=266&width=261&top_left_y=2252&top_left_x=1608)
12. A copper wire, 3 mm in diameter, is wound about a cylinder whose length is 12 cm , and diameter 10 cm , so as to cover the curved surface of the cylinder. Find the length and mass of the wire, assuming the density of copper to be 8.88 g per $\mathrm{cm}^{3}$.
13. A cistern, internally measuring $150 \mathrm{~cm} \times 120 \mathrm{~cm} \times 110 \mathrm{~cm}$, has $129600 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$ of water in it. Porous bricks are placed in the water until the cistern is full to the brim. Each brick absorbs one-seventeenth of its own volume of water. How many bricks can be put in without overflowing the water, each brick being $22.5 \mathrm{~cm} \times 7.5 \mathrm{~cm} \times 6.5 \mathrm{~cm}$ ?
14. In one fortnight of a given month, there was a rainfall of 10 cm in a river valley. If the area of the valley is $97280 \mathrm{~km}^{2}$, show that the total rainfall was approximately equivalent to the addition to the normal water of three rivers each 1072 km long, 75 m wide and 3 m deep.

## Exemplar Questions

1. A cone of maximum size is carved out from a cube of edge 14 cm . Find the surface area of the cone and of the remaining solid left out after the cone carved out.
2. A canal is 300 cm wide and 120 cm deep. The water in the canal is flowing with a speed of $20 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{h}$. How much area will it irrigate in 20 minutes if 8 cm of standing water is desired?
3. Three cubes of a metal whose edges are in the ratio $3: 4: 5$ are melted and converted into a single cube whose diagonal is $12 \sqrt{3} \mathrm{~cm}$. Find the edges of the three cubes.
4. A bucket is in the form of a frustum of a cone of height 30 cm with radii of its lower and upper ends as 10 cm and 20 cm , respectively. Find the capacity and surface area of the bucket. Also, find the cost of milk which can completely fill the container, at the rate of ₹ 25 per litre (use $\pi=3.14$ ).

## Exercise 3

Foundation Builder
5. If a marble of radius 2.1 cm is put into a cylindrical cup full of water of radius 5 cm and height 6 cm , then how much water flows out of the cylindrical cup?
(a) $38.8 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
(b) $55.4 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
(c) $19.4 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
(d) $471.4 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
6. A cubical ice-cream brick of edge 22 cm is to be distributed among some children by filling ice-cream cones of radius 2 cm and height 7 cm upto its brim. How many children will get the ice-cream cones?
(a) 163
(b) 263
(c) 363
(d) 463
7. The volume of the largest right circular cone that can be cut out from a cube of edge 4.2 cm is
(a) $9.7 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
(b) $77.6 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
(c) $58.2 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
(d) $19.4 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
8. If two solid hemispheres of same base radius $r$ are joined together along their bases, then curved surface area of this new solid is
(a) $4 \pi r^{2}$
(b) $6 \pi r^{2}$
(c) $3 \pi r^{2}$
(d) $8 \pi r^{2}$
9. A right circular cylinder of radius $r \mathrm{~cm}$ and height $h \mathrm{~cm} (h>2 r)$ just encloses a sphere of diameter
(a) $r \mathrm{~cm}$
(b) $2 r \mathrm{~cm}$
(c) $h \mathrm{~cm}$
(d) $2 h \mathrm{~cm}$
10. A pen stand made of wood is in the shape of a cuboid with four conical depressions to hold pens. The dimensions of the cuboid are 15 cm by 10 cm by 3.5 cm . The radius of each of the depressions is 0.5 cm and the depth is 1.4 cm .
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-402.jpg?height=481&width=698&top_left_y=1409&top_left_x=1122)

## Single Option Correct <br> DIRECTIONS : This section contains multiple choice questions. Each question has 4 choices (a), (b), (c) and (d) out of which only one is correct.

1. Three identical cones with base radius $r$ are placed on their bases so that each is touching the other two. The radius of the circle drawn through their vertices is -
(a) smaller than $r$
(b) equal to $r$
(c) larger than $r$
(d) depends on the height of the cones
2. The diameter of hollow cone is equal to the diameter of a spherical ball. If the ball is placed at the base of the cone, what portion of the ball will be outside the cone?
(a) $50 \%$
(b) less than $50 \%$
(c) more than $50 \%$
(d) $100 \%$
3. A slab of ice 8 inches in length, 11 inches in breadth, and 2 inches thick was melted and resolidified in the form of a rod of 8 inches diameter. The length of such a rod, in inches, is nearest to
(a) 3
(b) 3.5
(c) 4
(d) 4.5
4. The diagram shows the parts of a right cylinder. The volume of the cylinder, in $\mathrm{cm}^{3}$ is
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-403.jpg?height=92&width=108&top_left_y=2547&top_left_x=141)
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-403.jpg?height=340&width=325&top_left_y=376&top_left_x=726)
5. The base radii of a cone and a cylinder are equal. If their curved surface areas are also equal, then the ratio of the slant height of the cone to the height of the cylinder is
(a) $2: 1$
(b) $1: 2$
(c) $1: 3$
(d) $3: 1$
6. If the perimeter of one face of a cube is 20 cm , then its surface area is
(a) $120 \mathrm{~cm}^{2}$
(b) $150 \mathrm{~cm}^{2}$
(c) $125 \mathrm{~cm}^{2}$
(d) $400 \mathrm{~cm}^{2}$
7. A cube of side 12 cm is painted red on all the faces and then cut into smaller cubes, each of side 3 cm . What is the total number of smaller cubes having none of their faces painted?
(a) 16
(b) 8
(c) 12
(d) 24
8. If the diameter of the sphere is doubled, the surface area of the resultant sphere becomes $x$ times that of the original one, then $x$ would be
(a) 2
(b) 3
(c) 4
(d) 8
9. If $h$ be the height and $\alpha$ the semi-vertical angle of a right circular cone, then its volume is given by
(a) $\frac{1}{3} \pi h^{3} \tan ^{2} \alpha$
(b) $\frac{1}{3} \pi h^{2} \tan ^{2} \alpha$
(c) $\frac{1}{3} \pi h^{2} \tan ^{3} \alpha$
(d) $\frac{1}{3} \pi h^{3} \tan ^{3} \alpha$
10. If the radius of the sphere is increased by $100 \%$, the volume of the corresponding sphere is increased by
(a) $200 \%$
(b) $500 \%$
(c) $700 \%$
(d) $800 \%$
11. A sphere is melted and half of the melted liquid is used to form 11 identical cubes, whereas the remaining half is used to form 7 identical smaller spheres. The ratio of the side of the cube to the radius of the new small sphere is
(a) $\left(\frac{4}{3}\right)^{1 / 3}$
(b) $\left(\frac{8}{3}\right)^{1 / 3}$
(c) $(3)^{1 / 3}$
(d) 2
12. If a solid of one shape is converted to another, then the volume of the new solid
(a) remains same
(b) increases
(c) decreases
(d) can't say
13. In the adjoining figure, the bottom of the glass has a hemispherical raised portion. If the glass is filled with orange juice, the quantity of juice which a person will get is
(a) $135 \pi \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
(b) $117 \pi \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
(c) $99 \pi \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
(d) $36 \pi \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-403.jpg?height=377&width=166&top_left_y=2167&top_left_x=888)
14. Ratio of lateral surface areas of two cylinders with equal height is
(a) $1: 2$
(b) $\mathrm{H}: \mathrm{h}$
(c) $\mathrm{R}: \mathrm{r}$
(d) None of these
15. Ratio of volumes of two cylinders with equal height is
(a) $H: h$
(b) $R: r$
(c) $R^{2}: r^{2}$
(d) None of these
16. Ratio of volumes of two cones with same radii is
(a) $h_{1}: h_{2}$
(b) $s_{1}: s_{2}$
(c) $r_{1}: r_{2}$
(d) None of these
17. Volume of a spherical shell is given by
(a) $4 \pi\left(R^{2}-r^{2}\right)$
(b) $\pi\left(R^{3}-r^{3}\right)$
(c) $4 \pi\left(R^{3}-r^{3}\right)$
(d) $\frac{4}{3} \pi\left(R^{3}-r^{3}\right)$
18. The volume of a largest sphere that can be cut from cylindrical $\log$ of wood of base radius 1 m and height 4 m , is
(a) $\frac{16}{3} \neq \mathrm{m}^{3}$
(b) $\frac{8}{3} \neq \mathrm{m}^{3}$
(c) $\frac{4}{3} \neq \mathrm{m}^{3}$
(d) $\frac{10}{3} \neq \mathrm{m}^{3}$
19. If four times the sum of the areas of two circular faces of a cylinder of height 8 cm is equal to twice the curve surface area, then diameter of the cylinder is
(a) 4 cm
(b) 8 cm
(c) 2 cm
(d) 6 cm
20. A rectangular sheet of paper $40 \mathrm{~cm} \times 22 \mathrm{~cm}$, is rolled to form a hollow cylinder of height 40 cm . The radius of the cylinder (in cm) is
(a) 3.5
(b) 7
(c) $80 / 7$
(d) 5
21. A right circular cylinder has its height equal to two times its radius. It is inscribed in a right circular cone having its diameter equal to 10 cm and height 12 cm , and the axes of both the cylinder and the cone coincide. Then, the volume (in $\mathrm{cm}^{3}$ ) of the cylinder is approximately
[NTSE]
(a) 107.5
(b) 118.6
(c) 127.5
(d) 128.7
22. If the ratio of volumes of two cubes is $27: 64$, then the ratio of their surface area is:
(NTSE)
(a) $3: 4$
(b) $4: 3$
(c) $9: 16$
(d) $16: 9$
23. Volumes of two spheres are in the ratio $125: 64$. The ratio of their surface areas will be
[JSTSE]
(a) $5: 4$
(b) $25: 16$
(c) $16: 25$
(d) $125: 64$
24. A solid metallic block of volume one cubic metre is melted and recast into the form of a rectangular bar of length 9 metres having a square base. If the weight of the block is 90 kg and biggest cube is cut off from the bar, then the weight of the cube is
[NTSE]
(a) $6 \frac{1}{3} \mathrm{~kg}$
(b) $5 \frac{2}{3} \mathrm{~kg}$
(c) $4 \frac{2}{3} \mathrm{~kg}$
(d) $3 \frac{1}{3} \mathrm{~kg}$
25. Consider a cuboid all of whose edges are integers and whose base is a square. Suppose the sum of all its edges is numerically equal to the sum of the areas of all its six faces. Then, the sum of all its edges is
[KVPY]
(a) 12
(b) 18
(c) 24
(d) 36
26. Shyam wants to make a solid brick shape structure from 400 wooden cubes of unit volume each. If the sides of the solid brick have the ratio $1: 2: 3$, then the maximum number of cubes, which can be used, will be $\_\_\_\_$ .
[NTSE]
(a) 400
(b) 288
(c) 300
(d) 384
27. Sealed bottle containing some water is made up of two cylinders A and B of radius 1.5 cm and 3 cm respectively, as shown in the figure. When the bottle is placed right up on a table, the height of water intit is 15 cm , but when placed upside down the height of water is 24 cm . The height of the bottle is
[NTSE]
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-404.jpg?height=300&width=232&top_left_y=836&top_left_x=312)
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-404.jpg?height=298&width=232&top_left_y=861&top_left_x=648)
(a) 25 cm
(b) 26 cm
(c) 27 cm
(d) 28 cm
28. The number of solid cones with integer radius and integer height each having its volume numerically equal to its total surface area is
[KVPY]
(a) 0
(b) 1
(c) 2
(d) infinite
29. A solid metallic cylinder of height 10 cm and diameter 14 cm is melted to make two cones in the proportion of their volumes as $3: 4$, keeping the height 10 cm , what would be the percentage increase in the flat surface area?
[NTSE]
(a) 9
(b) 16
(c) 50
(d) 200
30. Given three cubes with integer side lengths, if the sum of surface areas of three cubes is $498 \mathrm{sq} . \mathrm{cm}$, then the sum of the volumes of the cubes in all possible solutions is
[NMTC]
(a) 731
(b) 495
(c) 1226
(d) None of these

## More Than One Option Correct

DIRECTIONS : This section contains multiple choice questions. Each question has 4 choices (a), (b), (c) and (d) out of which one or more may be correct.

1. Which one of the following is/are correct ?
(a) Total surface area of cuboid is $2(l b+b h+h l)$
(b) Total surface area of a cube is $6 l^{2}$
(c) Area of four walls $=2 h(l+b)$
(d) Area of four walls $=$ Height × Perimeter of the room
2. A Circular Cylinder can be separated into
(a) circular end at the bottom
(b) curved surface
(c) circular end at the top
(d) None of these
3. Which one of the following is / are correct ?
(a) Total surface area of cylinder is $2 \pi r^{2}+2 \pi r h$.
(b) Total surface area of a sphere is $4 \pi r^{2}$.
(c) Total surface area of cone is $\pi r^{2}+\pi r l$.
(d) None of these
4. Which one of the following is/ are made up of combinations of two or more of the basic solids?
(a) Buildings
(b) Top
(c) Monuments
(d) Test-tube
5. Among the following, which one is/are correct?
(a) The slant height is the longest side of a pyramid.
(b) The section between the base and a plane parallel to the base of a solid is known as frustum.
(c) All the surfaces of a cuboid are rectangular.
(d) For a cylinder, the top, the bottom and the walls of the cylinder determine the total surface area.
6. Which of the following statements is/are correct?
(a) The height or thickness of a frustrum is the perpendicular distance between its two circular bases.
(b) If a cone is cut by a plane parallel to the base of the cone, then the portion between the plane and the base is called 'frustum' of the cone.
(c) Frustum of a cone is the solid obtained after removing the upper portion of the cone by a plane parallel to its base.
(d) None of these

## Passage Based Questions

DIRECTIONS : Study the given paragraph(s) and answer the following questions.

## Passage-I

A test-tube is in the form of a right circular cylinder, surmounted by a cone. The diameter of the cylinder is 24 m . The height of the cylindrical portion is 11 m , while the vertex of the cone is 16 m above the ground.

1. The curved surface area of the cylindrical portion is
(a) $(246 \pi) \mathrm{m}^{2}$
(b) $(264 \pi) \mathrm{m}^{2}$
(c) $(426 \pi) \mathrm{m}^{2}$
(d) $(462 \pi) \mathrm{m}^{2}$
2. The slant height of the cone is
(a) 31 m
(b) 25 m
(c) 13 m
(d) 5 m
3. The area of the canvas required for the tent is
(a) $1320 \mathrm{~m}^{2}$
(b) $3120 \mathrm{~m}^{2}$
(c) $2130 \mathrm{~m}^{2}$
(d) $1230 \mathrm{~m}^{2}$

A semi-circular sheet of paper of diameter 28 cm is bent into an open conical cup. Then
4. The slant height of the cone is
(a) 2 cm
(b) 7 cm
(c) 14 cm
(d) 12 cm
5. The depth of the cup is.
(a) 12.12 cm
(b) 13 cm
(c) 11 cm
(d) 10 cm
6. The capacity of the cup is
(a) $623 \mathrm{~cm}^{2}$
(b) $622.16 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
(c) $621 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
(d) $620 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$

## Passage-IV

Wood is required to make a closed box of external length 1.2 m , breadth 90 cm and height 72 cm . The thickness of the walls of the box being 2 cm throughout.
9. The capacity of the box is
(a) $678638 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
(b) $678368 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
(c) $768368 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
(d) $768386 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
10. The volume of the wood used, is
(a) $99232 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
(b) $99322 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
(c) $99223 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
(d) $92932 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
11. The total cost of the wood at the rate of 20 paise per $\mathrm{cm}^{3}$ is
(a) ₹ 19864.40
(b) ₹ 19684.40
(c) ₹ 19486.40
(d) ₹ 19846.40

## Assertion \& Reason

DIRECTIONS : Each of these questions contains an Assertion followed by Reason. Read them carefully and answer the question on the basis of following options. You have to select the one that best describes the two statements.
(a) If both Assertion and Reason are correct and Reason is the correct explanation of Assertion.
(b) If both Assertion and Reason are correct, but Reason is not the correct explanation of Assertion.
(c) If Assertion is correct but Reason is incorrect.
(d) If Assertion is incorrect but Reason is correct.

1. Assertion: Total surface area of the cylinder having radius of the base 14 cm and height 30 cm is $3872 \mathrm{~cm}^{2}$.
2. Column-I (Object)
(A) Solids
(B) Road rollers
(C) Ice-cream cone
(D) Volleyball

Column-II (Shape)
(p) Right circular cone
(q) Sphere
(r) Cylinder
(s) Cuboid
(t) Cube

Reason: If $r$ be the radius and $h$ be the height of the cylinder, then total surface area $=\left(2 \pi r h+2 \pi r^{2}\right)$.
2. Assertion: If the height of a cone is 24 cm and diameter of the base is 14 cm , then the slant height of the cone is 15 cm .
Reason: If $r$ be the radius and $h$ the slant height of the cone, then slant height $=\sqrt{h^{2}+r^{2}}$.
3. Assertion: If the radius of a cone is halved and volume is not changed, then height remains same.
Reason: If the radius of a cone is halved and volume is not changed then height must become four times of the original height.
4. Assertion: If a ball is in the shape of a sphere has a surface area of $221.76 \mathrm{~cm}^{2}$, then its diameter is 8.4 cm .
Reason: If the radius of the sphere be $r$, then surface area, $\mathrm{S}=4 \pi r^{2}$, i. e. $r=\frac{1}{2} \sqrt{\frac{S}{\pi}}$.
5. Assertion: No. of spherical balls that can be made out of a solid cube of lead whose edge is 44 cm , each ball being 4 cm . in diameter, is 2541

Reason : Number of balls $=\frac{\text { Volume of one ball }}{\text { volume of lead }}$.

## Multiple Matching Question

## DIRECTIONS : Following question has four statements $(A, B$, $C$ and $D)$ given in Column-I and statements ( $p, q, r, s$ and $t$ ) in Column-II. Any given statement in Column-I can have correct matching with one or more statement(s) given in Column-II.

3. If a cone, a hemisphere and a cylinder have equal bases and have same height, then the ratio of their volumes is $1: 2: k$. The value of ' $k$ ' is
4. A cone is of height 24 cm and the base radius 9 cm . Find the radius of the circular section cut from the cone by a plane parallel to the base and 8 cm from it.

## SOLUTIONS

## (Brief Explanations of Selected Questions)

## Multiple Choice Questions

1. (b) Volume of cylinder $=\pi r^{2} h$

$$
\begin{aligned}
& 448 \pi=\pi r^{2} \times 7 \\
\Rightarrow & r^{2}=\frac{448}{7} \Rightarrow r=\sqrt{\frac{448}{7}} \\
\Rightarrow & r=\sqrt{64} \Rightarrow r=8 \mathrm{~cm}
\end{aligned}
$$

∴ L. S. A or C.S. A $=2 \pi h$

$$
=2 \times \frac{22}{7} \times 8 \times 7=352 \mathrm{~cm}^{2}
$$

2. (a) $r=\frac{1.4}{2}=0.7 \mathrm{~m}$ and $h=2 \mathrm{~m}$

Area covered $=$ C.S.A × number of revolutions

$$
\begin{aligned}
& =2 \pi h \times 5=10 \pi r h \\
\Rightarrow & 10\left(\frac{22}{7}\right)(0.7)(2)=44 \mathrm{~m}^{2}
\end{aligned}
$$

3. (d) Let the diameter of the sphere be d units and the edge of the cube be a units, then

$$
4 \pi\left(\frac{d}{2}\right)^{2}=6 a^{2} \Rightarrow \frac{d^{2}}{a^{2}}=\frac{6}{\pi} \Rightarrow \frac{d}{a}=\frac{\sqrt{6}}{\sqrt{\pi}} .
$$

4. (b) In triangle OVB and $\mathrm{PVC}, \triangle \mathrm{OVB} \sim \triangle \mathrm{PVC}$
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-407.jpg?height=412&width=396&top_left_y=1651&top_left_x=452)

Therefore,

$$
\frac{\mathrm{OB}}{\mathrm{PC}}=\frac{\mathrm{VO}}{\mathrm{PV}} \Rightarrow \frac{28}{7}=\frac{h+45}{h}
$$

(corresponding sides are in same ratio)
$\Rightarrow \quad \frac{h+45}{h}=\frac{4}{1} \Rightarrow h+45=4 h$
$\Rightarrow 3 h=45 \Rightarrow h=15 \mathrm{~cm}$
Capacity of bucket i.e, volume of bucket
= Volume of cone VBA - Volume of cone VCE

$$
=\frac{1}{3} \pi(28)^{2}(45+15)-\frac{1}{3} \pi(7)^{2} \times 15=48510 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}
$$

5. (a) $R=\frac{45.5}{2}=22.75 \mathrm{~cm}, r=\frac{42}{2}=21 \mathrm{~cm}$

Capacity (volume)

$$
\begin{aligned}
& =\frac{2}{3} \pi\left(\mathrm{R}^{3}-\mathrm{r}^{3}\right)=\frac{2}{3} \times \frac{22}{7} \times\left[(22.75)^{3}-(21)^{3}\right] \\
& =\frac{2}{3} \times \frac{22}{7} \times 2513.543=5266.46 \mathrm{~cm}^{3} \\
\text { or } & =\frac{5266.46}{1000} \text { lts. }=5.27 \text { litres }
\end{aligned}
$$

Outer surface area

$$
=2 \pi \mathrm{R}^{2}=2 \times \frac{22}{7} \times(22.75)^{2}=3253.25 \mathrm{~cm}^{2}
$$

6. (c) We know that, $\ell=\sqrt{r^{2}+h^{2}}$,

$$
v=\frac{1}{3} \pi r^{2} h, c=\pi r \ell
$$

L.H.S. $3 \pi v h^{3}-c^{2} h^{2}+9 v^{2}$
$\Rightarrow 3 \pi\left(\frac{1}{3} \pi r^{2} h\right) h^{3}-(\pi r \ell)^{2} h^{2}+9\left(\frac{1}{3} \pi r^{2} h\right)^{2}$
$\Rightarrow 3 \pi\left(\frac{1}{3} \pi r^{2} h\right) h^{3}-\pi^{2} r^{2}\left(r^{2}+h^{2}\right)$
$h^{2}+9 \times \frac{1}{9} \pi^{2} r^{4} h^{2}$

$$
\left(\because \quad \ell=\sqrt{h^{2}+r^{2}}\right)
$$

$\Rightarrow \pi^{2} r^{2} h^{4}-\pi^{2} r^{4} h^{2}-\pi^{2} r^{2} h^{4}+\pi^{2} r^{4} h^{2}=0=$ R.H.S.
7. (b) Height of cylinder, $h=2 \mathrm{~cm}$

Circumference of base of cylinder $=10 \mathrm{~cm}$
$\Rightarrow 2 \pi r=10 \Rightarrow r=\frac{5}{\pi}$
Volume of the cylinder $=\pi r^{2} h=\pi \times \frac{25}{\pi^{2}} \times 2=\frac{50}{\pi}$
8. (a) In right $\triangle \mathrm{AOB}$
$\frac{\mathrm{OB}}{\mathrm{OA}}=\tan \alpha \Rightarrow \mathrm{OB}=h \tan \alpha$
$\Rightarrow r=h \tan \alpha$
Volume of cone $=\frac{1}{3} \pi r^{2} h$
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-407.jpg?height=261&width=262&top_left_y=1937&top_left_x=1612)

$$
=\frac{1}{3} \pi(h \tan \alpha)^{2} h=\frac{1}{3} \pi h^{3} \tan ^{2} \alpha
$$

9. (d) Volume of $A=18^{3}=5832 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$

Volume of $B=24^{3}=13824 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
Volume of $C=27000 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
Total volume $A, B$ and $C=46656 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
Now, volume of new cube $D=$ sum of volumes of $A, B$ and $C$.
Let a be the edge of $D$.
Then $a^{3}=46656 \Rightarrow a=36 \mathrm{~cm}$.
10. (b) $\pi r^{2}=346.5 \Rightarrow r^{2}=\left(\frac{3465}{10} \times \frac{7}{22}\right)$

$$
\begin{aligned}
& =\frac{441}{4}=\left(\frac{21}{2}\right)^{2} \Rightarrow r=\frac{21}{2} \mathrm{~m} \\
l & =\sqrt{r^{2}+h^{2}}=\sqrt{\frac{441}{4}}+(14)^{2}=\sqrt{\frac{1225}{4}}=\frac{35}{2} \mathrm{~m}
\end{aligned}
$$

Area of canvas

$$
=\pi r l=\left(\frac{22}{7} \times \frac{21}{2} \times \frac{35}{2}\right) \mathrm{m}^{2}=577.5 \mathrm{~m}^{2}
$$

Length of canvas $=\frac{\text { Area }}{\text { Width }}=\frac{577.50}{1.1} \mathrm{~m}=525 \mathrm{~m}$

## Very Short Answer Questions

1. Volume of the tank which is in the form of a cuboid $=l \times b \times h=483884 \mathrm{~m}^{3}$
2. Volume of cuboid $=(18 \times 12 \times 9) \mathrm{m}^{3}$

Volume of each cube $=(3)^{3}=27 \mathrm{~m}^{3}$
Let the required no. of cubes be $n$, then
$n(27)=18 \times 12 \times 9$
$n=72$.
3. Circumference of the cylinder
$=$ length of the rectangular paper base radius $=\frac{3}{\pi} \mathrm{~cm}$
4. Old Volume $=L B H$,

New Volume $=2 L \times 2.5 \mathrm{~B} \times 3.5 ~ H=17.5 L B H$
⇒ \% increase in volume $=\frac{17.5 L B H-L B H}{L B H} \times 100$
$=1650 \%$.
5. $1: 2$
6. Radius of base, $r=7 \mathrm{~m}$, vertical height, $h=24 \mathrm{~m}$

Slant height, $l=\sqrt{h^{2}+r^{2}}=25 \mathrm{~m}$
Curved surface area $=\pi r l=550 \mathrm{~m}^{2}$
Width of cloth $=5 \mathrm{~m}$
∴ Length required to make conical tent

$$
=\frac{550}{5} \mathrm{~m}=110 \mathrm{~m} .
$$

7. Area of the cardboard required $=$ curved surface area of the cap (or cone) $=\pi r l$
$\therefore \quad$ Area of the cardboard required $=550 \mathrm{~cm}^{2}$
8. Ratio of the radii of two cylinders $=2: 1$

Ratio of height $=1: 2$
Let the radii be $2 r$ and $r$, and the height be $h$ and $2 h$.
$\therefore \quad \frac{\text { Volume of 1st cylinder }}{\text { Volume of 2nd cylinder }}=\frac{(2 r)^{2} h}{r^{2}(2 h)}=\frac{4 r^{2} h}{2 r^{2} h}=\frac{2}{1}$
⇒ Ratio of volumes $=2: 1$
9. The ratio of the curved surfaces of two cones $=2: 1$

The ratio of their slant heights $=1: 2$
Curved surface of first cone $=\pi r_{1} \ell_{1}$
Curved surface of second cone $=\pi r_{2} \ell_{2}$
$\therefore \quad \frac{\pi r_{1} \ell_{1}}{\pi r_{2} \ell_{2}}=\frac{2}{1} \Rightarrow \frac{r_{1}}{r_{2}} \times \frac{\ell}{2 \ell}=\frac{2}{1} \Rightarrow \frac{r_{1}}{r_{2}}=\frac{4}{1}$
or $r_{1}: r_{2}=4: 1$
10. $282.333 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
11. $2 \pi r=4, r=7 \mathrm{~cm}, V=\pi \times 7 \times 7 \times 18=2772 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$.
12. Volume of metallic sphere $=\frac{4}{3} \pi\left[(4)^{3}-(2)^{3}\right]$
= Volume of cone.
13. $\frac{V_{1}}{V_{2}}=\frac{\frac{4}{3} \pi(8)^{3}}{\frac{4}{3} \pi(1)^{3}}=\frac{8 \times 8 \times 8}{1 \times 1 \times 1}=512$ balls
14. Since height of cylinder $=$ radius (i.e., $h=r$ )

Volume $=\pi r^{2} h$
$25 \frac{1}{7}=\frac{22}{7} \times h^{2} \times h$
$\frac{176}{7}=\frac{22}{7} \times h^{3}$
$h^{3}=\frac{176}{7} \times \frac{7}{22}=8$
$h=2 \mathrm{~cm}$

## Short Answer Questions

1. Diameter $=126 \mathrm{~m}, \quad \therefore$ Radius $(r)=63 \mathrm{~m}$,

Height of the cylinder $(h)=5 \mathrm{~m}$, Total height $=21 \mathrm{~m}$
∴ Height of the conical portion, $H=21-5=16 \mathrm{~m}$
Curved surface area of cylinder $=2 \pi r h=1980$ sq. m
Lateral surface area of cone $=\pi r \ell$
∴ Lateral surface area of cone

$$
=\frac{22}{7} \times 63 \times \sqrt{(16)^{2}+(63)^{2}}=12870 \text { sq. cm. }
$$

∴ Required cost $=12 \times(1980+12870)=₹ 178200$
2. Volume of $A=18^{3}=5832 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$

Volume of $B=24^{3}=13824 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
Volume of $C=27000 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
Total volume of $A, B$ and $C=46656 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$

Now, volume of new cube $D=$ sum of volumes of $A, B$ and $C$.
Let $a$ be the edge of $D$.
Then, $a^{3}=46656$
$\Rightarrow a=36 \mathrm{~cm}$.
3. $r: h=3: 4$.
4. Volume of Wire = Volume of Cylinder

⇒ Volume $=\pi r^{2} h=\pi(d / 2)^{2} h$
$\Rightarrow($ Old $V)=($ New $V)$
$r=\frac{19 d}{40}$
∴ New $V=\pi \times \frac{19 d}{40} \times \frac{19 d}{40} \times h$
Hence, percentage change in length is $10.8 \%$.
5. Let radius of the sphere be $r$. An increase by $100 \%$ makes radius $=2 r$
Percent increase in volume
$=\frac{V_{2}-V_{1}}{V_{1}} \times 100 \%=\frac{\left(\frac{32}{3}-\frac{4}{3}\right) \pi r^{3}}{\frac{4}{3} \pi r^{3}} \times 100=700 \%$
6. $r=1 \mathrm{~cm}$.
7. Since, diameters of two cones are equal, therefore their radii are also equal. Let the radius of each cone be $r$. Again, since their slant heights are in the ratio $5: 4$, therefore let their slant heights be $5 l$ and $4 l$ respectively.
∴ Curved surface area of first cone $=\pi \mathrm{r}(5 l)=5 \pi \mathrm{r} l$ and curved surface area of second cone $=\pi r(4 l)=4 \pi r l$
∴ Ratio of their curved surface areas

$$
=\frac{5 \pi r l}{4 \pi r l}=\frac{5}{4}=5: 4
$$

8. When the sphere exactly fit inside the cube, it is the largest sphere. Then,
Radius $r$ of the largest sphere $=\frac{1}{2} a$, where $a$ is the side of the cube.
Now, volume of cube $=a^{3}$ cubic units
Volume of sphere $=\frac{4}{3} \pi\left(\frac{a}{2}\right)^{3}$ cubic units $\frac{\pi a^{3}}{6}$
∴ Required ratio $=6: \pi$
9. The total volume of the haystack = Volume of the cone + Volume of inverted frustrum

$$
=\frac{1}{3} \pi R^{2} H+\frac{\pi h}{3}\left(R^{2}+r^{2}+R r\right)
$$

Here, $R=A B=3 \mathrm{~m} ; r=C D=2 \mathrm{~m}$
$H=$ Height of cone $=7 \mathrm{~m}$
$h=$ height of the frustrum $=(10.5-7) \mathrm{m}=3.5 \mathrm{~cm}$
∴ The total volume of the haystack

$$
\begin{aligned}
& =\frac{1}{3} \pi(3)^{2} \times 7+\frac{1}{3} \pi(3.5)\left[3^{2}+2^{2}+(3 \times 2)\right] \\
& =\frac{407}{3}=135.90 \mathrm{~m}^{3} .
\end{aligned}
$$

10. (a) There is $125 \%$ increase in the surface area of the cube.
(b) $3: 2$
11. Diameter of tent $=7 \mathrm{~m} \quad \therefore$ Radius of tent $=7 / 2 \mathrm{~m} h=6 \mathrm{~m}$.
∴ Volume of tent $=\frac{1}{3} \pi r^{2} h$

$$
=\frac{1}{3} \times \frac{22}{7} \times\left(\frac{7}{2}\right)^{2} \times(6)=77 \mathrm{~m}^{3}
$$

Air space required per head soldier $=77 / 12=6.4 \mathrm{~m}^{3}$
12. ∵ Volume of liquid in the hemispherical bowl
$=\frac{2}{3} \times \pi \times(18)^{3} \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
So, volume (after wastage) $=\frac{2 \pi}{3} \times(18)^{3} \times \frac{90}{100} \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
Now, volume of liquid in 72 bottles $=\pi(3)^{2} \times h \times 72 \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$
Hence, height $=\frac{\frac{2}{3} \pi(18)^{3} \times \frac{9}{10}}{\pi(3)^{2} \times 72}=5.4 \mathrm{~cm}$
13. Increase in the height of water level in the cylindrical vessel due to sphere $(h)=32 / 9 \mathrm{~cm}$
Radius of the sphere $(R)=6 \mathrm{~cm}$
Suppose radius of the cylindrical vessel be $r$.
Volume of water in cylinder = Volume of sphere

$$
\begin{aligned}
& \pi r^{2} h=\frac{4}{3} \pi R^{3} \Rightarrow r^{2}=\frac{4 \times 6^{3} \times 9}{3 \times 32} \\
\Rightarrow & r=\sqrt{27 \times 3}=9 \mathrm{~cm}
\end{aligned}
$$

Hence, diameter of the cylindrical vessel $=18 \mathrm{~cm}$.
14. The circumference of circular end $\mathrm{ABA}=18 \mathrm{~cm}$

So, $2 \pi \mathrm{R}=18$

$$
\mathrm{R}=\frac{9}{\pi}
$$

Now, the circumference of circular end CDC $=6 \mathrm{~cm}$ Therefore,
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-409.jpg?height=241&width=314&top_left_y=1635&top_left_x=1557)

$$
\begin{aligned}
& 2 \pi r=6 \\
& r=\frac{3}{\pi}
\end{aligned}
$$

As, $\triangle \mathrm{PED}$ and $\triangle \mathrm{PFB}$ are similar.
Then, $\frac{x}{r}=\frac{x+4}{R}$
$\Rightarrow \frac{x}{3}=\frac{x+4}{9} \Rightarrow 3 x=x+4$
$\Rightarrow 2 x=4 \Rightarrow x=2 \mathrm{~cm}$
Thus, curved surface area of the frustum

$$
\begin{aligned}
& =\pi \mathrm{R}(4+x)-\pi r x=\pi \times \frac{9}{\pi} \times 6-\pi \times \frac{3}{\pi} \times 2 \\
& =54-6=48 \mathrm{~cm}^{2}
\end{aligned}
$$

Hence, curved surface area of the frustum is $48 \mathrm{~cm}^{2}$.
15.
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-410.jpg?height=300&width=561&top_left_y=379&top_left_x=269)

Let width and height of the canal,
$w=6 \mathrm{~cm} ; h=1.5 \mathrm{~m}$
Speed $=10 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{hr}$
Height of the field $=8 \mathrm{~cm}=0.08 \mathrm{~m}$.
∵ Water flow in 1 hour $=10 \mathrm{~km}$
i.e., water flow in 60 minute $=10,000$ meter
∴ water flow in 1 minute $=\frac{10,000}{60}$ meter
∴ water flow in 30 minutes $=\frac{10,000}{60} \times 30=5000$ meter.
Since, volume of water in canal $=$ Volume of water in field.
∵ Both are cuboidal in shape.
$\therefore \quad \mathrm{V}=l b h_{1}$.
where, $l=$ length of the field,
$b=$ width of the field,
$h_{1}=$ height of the field.
Hence, $5000 \times 6 \times 1.5=l \times b \times 0.08$
$\Rightarrow \frac{5,000 \times 6 \times 1.5}{0.08}=l \times b$
$562500=$ Area of the field.
Therefore, irrigated area in 30 minutes $=562500 \mathrm{~m}^{2}$
or 56.25 hectares

![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-411.jpg?height=316&width=289&top_left_y=2156&top_left_x=762)

## Recycling

Recycled water can be used in irrigation specially in those areas where yearly rainfall average is less. Recycled water can also be used to increase ground water level and different human needs that are depended on water.

$$
r_{2}=\frac{10}{2}=5 \mathrm{~cm}

\begin{aligned}
& =\pi l\left(r_{1}+r_{2}\right)+\pi r_{2}^{2}=3.14 \times 26 \times(15+5)+3.14 \times 5 \times 5 \\

## Reasoning Based Questions

1. Let $a$ be the side of base and $h$ be the height of prism. We know that volume of prism $=$ Area of base × height
$\Rightarrow 80 \sqrt{3}=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4} a^{2} \times h$
$\left(\because\right.$ Area of equilateral triangle $\left.=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4} a^{2}\right)$
Lateral surface of prism $=3 a(h)=240$

Dividing eq. (i) by (ii), we get
$\frac{\sqrt{3}}{12} a=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{3} \Rightarrow a=4$
From eq. (ii), $h=20$
2. It is given that CSA of conical cup is $154 \sqrt{2} \mathrm{~cm}^{2}$ and radius ( $r$ ) is 7 cm .
CSA of conical cup $=\pi r l$
$\Rightarrow 154 \sqrt{2}=\frac{22}{7} \times 7 \times l$
$\Rightarrow l=7 \sqrt{2} \mathrm{~cm}$ and $h=7 \mathrm{~cm}$
∴ By using formula, $\tan \left(\frac{\theta}{2}\right)=\frac{r}{h}$
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-412.jpg?height=318&width=254&top_left_y=950&top_left_x=740)

Where $\theta$ is angle at vertex of cup.
$\tan \left(\frac{\theta}{2}\right)=\frac{7}{7}=l \Rightarrow \tan \frac{\theta}{2}=\tan 45^{\circ} \Rightarrow \theta=90^{\circ}$
3. Since, $\triangle \mathrm{CED}$ is equilateral triangle.
∴ Area of $\triangle \mathrm{CED}\left(\mathrm{A}_{1}\right)$
$=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4} \times 4 \times 4=4 \sqrt{3} \mathrm{~cm}^{2}$
Now, Area of $\triangle \mathrm{AED}\left(\mathrm{A}_{2}\right)$
$=\frac{1}{2} \cdot \mathrm{AD} \cdot \mathrm{ED} \cdot \sin \angle \mathrm{ADE}$
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-412.jpg?height=291&width=366&top_left_y=1452&top_left_x=628)

Here, $\mathrm{AD}=\mathrm{DE}=4 \mathrm{~cm}$
$\therefore \quad \angle \mathrm{ADE}=90^{\circ}-\angle \mathrm{CDE}=30^{\circ}$
So, $\mathrm{A}_{2}=\frac{1}{2} \times 4 \times 4 \times \sin 30^{\circ}=4 \mathrm{~cm}^{2}$
Area of $\triangle \mathrm{ADC}\left(\mathrm{A}_{3}\right)=\frac{1}{2} \times 4 \times 4=8 \mathrm{~cm}^{2}$
$\therefore \quad$ Area of $\triangle \mathrm{AEC}=\mathrm{A}_{1}+\mathrm{A}_{2}-\mathrm{A}_{3}=4 \sqrt{3}+4-8$
$=4(\sqrt{3}-1) \mathrm{cm}^{2}$
So, area of $\triangle \mathrm{ACE}$ is $4(\sqrt{3}-1) \mathrm{cm}^{2}$.
4. Since, $4 \sqrt{2}$ is diagonal of each cube
$\therefore \quad$ side of each cube $=\frac{4 \sqrt{2}}{\sqrt{2}}=4 \mathrm{~cm}$
and Side of other three cubes $=\frac{8 \sqrt{2}}{\sqrt{2}}=8 \mathrm{~cm}$
Let the side of the new cube formed be $a \mathrm{~cm}$.
So, total volume of six cubes $=3\left((4)^{3}+(8)^{3}\right)$

Now, $l^{2}=h^{2}+(R-r)^{2}$
$\Rightarrow l=\sqrt{63.09}=7.94$

![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-412.jpg?height=383&width=317&top_left_y=1219&top_left_x=1500)
tumbler which is in the form of a frustum of cone

2. On rolling up the sector, we get a cone whose

Slant height = radius of sector = 6 cm .
Circumference of the base of cone $=$ length of sector
$2 \pi r=\frac{\pi r \theta}{180^{\circ}}=\frac{\pi \times 6 \times 120^{\circ}}{180^{\circ}}=4 \pi \mathrm{~cm}$
$\Rightarrow r=2 \mathrm{~cm}$
Total S.A of cone = C.S. A. of cone

+ area of base of cone.
$=$ Area of sector $+\pi(2)^{2}$
![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-412.jpg?height=247&width=246&top_left_y=1962&top_left_x=1571)
$=\frac{\pi r^{2} \theta}{360^{\circ}}+4 \pi=\frac{\pi(6)^{2} \times 120^{\circ}}{360^{\circ}}+4 \pi=16 \pi \mathrm{~cm}^{2}$
$h=\sqrt{\ell^{2}-r^{2}}=\sqrt{36-4}=\sqrt{32}=4 \sqrt{2} \mathrm{~cm}$.
Volume of cone $=\frac{1}{3} \pi(2)^{2} \times(4 \sqrt{2})=\frac{16 \pi \sqrt{2}}{3} \mathrm{~cm}^{3}$

$$
=\frac{4}{3} \pi\left(\frac{5}{2}\right)^{3}=\frac{1375}{21} \mathrm{~cm}^{3}
$$

![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/9afb5168-3a1c-43f6-9248-79825029b395-413.jpg?height=273&width=250&top_left_y=379&top_left_x=735)
